parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Object-based land cover mapping
Agricultural field delineation from imagery
Urban morphology analysis
Preprocessing for machine learning classification
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }

    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines

parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

brief_description: Creates a raster showing the cumulative cost of moving from each cell to the nearest source cell, using a cost surface.
full_description: Computes the cumulative cost of moving across a raster landscape from each cell to one or more source cells, considering a friction (cost) surface. Used for least-cost path analysis.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input raster layer to reproject.

parameters.name: SOURCE_CRS
parameters.label:
Source 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: Defines the CRS of the input raster layer.

parameters.name: TARGET_CRS
parameters.label:
Target 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: The CRS of the output layer.

parameters.name: RESAMPLING
parameters.label: Resampling method to use
parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

brief_description: Fills sinks in a raster digital elevation model to create a hydrologically conditioned DEM.
full_description: Fills depressions (sinks) in a DEM so that water can flow continuously downhill. Essential preprocessing step before watershed delineation.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input raster layer to reproject.

parameters.name: SOURCE_CRS
parameters.label:
Source 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: Defines the CRS of the input raster layer.

parameters.name: TARGET_CRS
parameters.label:
Target 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: The CRS of the output layer.

parameters.name: RESAMPLING
parameters.label: Resampling method to use
parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: DEM preprocessing for hydrologic analysis
Removing artifacts from elevation data
Enabling proper flow routing
Conditioning DEMs from LIDAR or SRTM

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer

parameters.name: SOURCE_CRS
parameters.label:
Source 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: Defines the CRS of the input raster layer.

parameters.name: TARGET_CRS
parameters.label:
Target 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: The CRS of the output layer.

parameters.name: RESAMPLING
parameters.label: Resampling method to use
parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

parameters.label: Resampling method to use
parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.description: Input raster layer to reproject.

parameters.name: SOURCE_CRS
parameters.label:
Source 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: Defines the CRS of the input raster layer.

parameters.name: TARGET_CRS
parameters.label:
Target 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: The CRS of the output layer.

parameters.name: RESAMPLING
parameters.label: Resampling method to use
parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.label: Resampling method to use
parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }

    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems

parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def reproject_raster():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")
    
                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'SOURCE_CRS': '',
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',
        'RESAMPLING': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
        'NODATA': '',
        'TARGET_RESOLUTION': '',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPTIONS': '',
        'DATA_TYPE': 0,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
        'TARGET_EXTENT': '',
        'TARGET_EXTENT_CRS': '',
        'MULTITHREADING': '',
        'EXTRA': '''    }
                                      
    result = processing.run("gdal:warpreproject", parameters)
                                       
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(output_path, 'Reprojected Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
reproject_raster()

full_description: GRASS GIS tool: r.relief. Refer to GRASS documentation for full details.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input raster layer to reproject.

parameters.name: SOURCE_CRS
parameters.label:
Source 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: Defines the CRS of the input raster layer.

parameters.name: TARGET_CRS
parameters.label:
Target 
CRS Optional
parameters.description: The CRS of the output layer.

parameters.name: RESAMPLING
parameters.label: Resampling method to use
parameters.description: Pixel value resampling method to use.Options: 0 — Nearest Neighbour 1 — Bilinear (2x2 kernel) 2 — Cubic (4x4 kernel) 3 — Cubic B-Spline (4x4 kernel) 4 — Lanczos (6x6 kernel) 5 — Average 6 — Mode 7 — Maximum 8 — Minimum 9 — Median 10 — First quartile (Q1) 11 — Third quartile (Q3).

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: NoData value for output bands Optional
parameters.description: Sets NoData value for output bands.If not provided, then NoData values will be copied fromthe source dataset..

parameters.name: TARGET_RESOLUTION
parameters.label: Output file resolution in target georeferenced units Optional
parameters.description: Defines the output file resolution of reprojection result.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Reprojected
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OPTIONS
parameters.label: Additional creation options Optional
parameters.description: For adding one or more creation options that control theraster to be created (colors, block size, filecompression…).For convenience, you can rely on predefined profiles (see GDAL driver options section ). Batch Process and Model Designer: separate multiple options with a pipecharacter ( | )..

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Defines the format of the output raster file.Options: 0 — Use Input Layer Data Type 1 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 2 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 3 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Int32 (Thi

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT
parameters.label: Georeferenced extents of output file to be created Optional
parameters.description: Sets the georeferenced extent of the output file to becreated (in the Target CRS by default.In the CRS of the target raster extent , ifspecified). Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout ma

parameters.name: TARGET_EXTENT_CRS
parameters.label: CRS of the target raster extent Optional
parameters.description: Specifies the CRS in which to interpret the coordinates givenfor the extent of the output file.This must not be confused with the target CRS of the outputdataset.It is instead a convenience e.g. when knowing the outputcoordinates in a geodetic long/lat CRS, but wanting a resultin a projected coordin

parameters.name: MULTITHREADING
parameters.label: Use multithreaded warping implementation
parameters.description: Two threads will be used to process chunks of the image andperform input/output operations simultaneously.Note that the computation itself is not multithreaded..

parameters.name: EXTRA
parameters.label: Additional command-line parameters Optional
parameters.description: Add extra GDAL command line options..
