import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject

def create_random_binomial_raster_layer():
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    parameters = {
        'EXTENT': 'xmin,ymin,xmax,ymax', # Specify extent: use map canvas extent, calculate from layer, etc.
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326', # Example CRS, options are: Project CRS, layer CRS, etc.
        'PIXEL_SIZE': 1.0,
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OUTPUT_TYPE': 0, # Output types: 0 — Integer16, 1 — Unsigned Integer16, 2 — Integer32, 3 — Unsigned Integer32, 4 — Float32, 5 — Float64
        'N': 10,
        'PROBABILITY': 0.5
    }
    
    result = processing.run("""native:createrandombinomialrasterlayer", parameters)
    raster_layer = QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(result['OUTPUT'])

create_random_binomial_raster_layer()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject

def create_random_gamma_raster(output_path):
    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'EXTENT': 'xmin,xmax,ymin,ymax',  # Specify your coordinates
        'TARGET_CRS': 'ProjectCrs',  # Define desired CRS
        'PIXEL_SIZE': 1.0,  # Default pixel size
        'OUTPUT': output_path,  # Output path
        'OUTPUT_TYPE': 0,  # Output raster data type options: ['0': 'Float32', '1': 'Float64']
        'ALPHA': 1.0,  # Default alpha value
        'BETA': 1.0  # Default beta value
    }

    # Run the algorithm
    result = processing.run("""native:createrandomgammarasterlayer", parameters)
                            
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Random_Gamma_Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                         
output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
create_random_gamma_raster(output_path)

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject

def create_random_negative_binomial_raster(output_path):
    parameters = {
        'EXTENT': 'Use map canvas extent',  # Options: 'Calculate from layer...', 'Calculate from layout map...', 'Calculate from bookmark...', 'Use map canvas extent', 'Draw on canvas', 'Enter the coordinates as xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax'
        'TARGET_CRS': 'Project CRS',
        'PIXEL_SIZE': 1.0,
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OUTPUT_TYPE': 0,  # Options: 0 — Integer16, 1 — Unsigned Integer16, 2 — Integer32, 3 — Unsigned Integer32, 4 — Float32, 5 — Float64
        'K_PARAMETER': 10,
        'PROBABILITY': 0.5
    }
    
    result = processing.run("""native:createrandomnegativebinomialrasterlayer", parameters)
    
    output_layer = QgsProject.instance().mapLayersByName('Random Negative Binomial')[0]
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsRasterLayer

def create_random_normal_raster_layer(output_path):
    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'EXTENT': 'xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax',  # Options: Calculate from layer, Calculate from layout map, Calculate from bookmark, Use map canvas extent, Draw on canvas
        'TARGET_CRS': QgsProject.instance().crs(),  # Default: Project CRS
        'PIXEL_SIZE': 1.0,  # Default: 1.0
        'OUTPUT': output_path,  # Save to a Temporary File, Save to File
        'OUTPUT_TYPE': 0,  # Options: 0 — Float32, 1 — Float64
        'MEAN': 0.0,  # Default: 0.0
        'STDDEV': 1.0,  # Default: 1.0
    }
    # Run the algorithm
    result = processing.run("""native:createrandomnormalrasterlayer", parameters)
                                  
    output_raster = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'RandomNormalRasterLayer')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_raster)

                                            
output_raster_path = "path/to/input_file"
create_random_normal_raster_layer(output_raster_path)

brief_description: Generates a raster layer for given extent and cell size filled with random values.
full_description:
Generates a raster layer for given extent and cell size filled with random values.
By default, the values will range between the minimum and maximum value of the specified output raster type. This can be overridden by using the advanced parameters for lower and upper bound value. If the bounds have the same value or both are zero (default) the algorithm will create random values in the full value range of the chosen raster data type. Choosing bounds outside the acceptable range of the output raster type will abort the algorithm.

parameters.name: EXTENT
parameters.label: Desired extent
parameters.description: Specify the spatial extent of the output raster layer.It will internally be extended to a multiple of the tile size. Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout map item in the active project C

parameters.name: TARGET_CRS
parameters.label:
Target 
CRS
parameters.description: CRS for the output raster layer.

parameters.name: PIXEL_SIZE
parameters.label: Pixel size
parameters.description: Pixel size (X=Y) in map units..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output raster
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster. One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: LOWER_BOUND
parameters.label: Lower bound for random number range

parameters.name: UPPER_BOUND
parameters.label: Upper bound for random number range

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
from qgis.core import QgsProject
import processing

def generate_random_raster():
    output_path = """path/to/input_file"
    
    parameters = {
        'EXTENT': '10,20,10,20',                                   
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',                      
        'PIXEL_SIZE': 1.0,
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OUTPUT_TYPE': 5,                                                      
        'LOWER_BOUND': 0.0,
        'UPPER_BOUND': 1.0
    }
    
    result = processing.run("native:createrandomuniformrasterlayer", parameters)
    
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Random_Raster', 'gdal')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

generate_random_raster()

brief_description: Clusters point features based on a 2D implementation of Density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN) algorithm.
full_description:
Clusters point features based on a 2D implementation of Density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN) algorithm.
The algorithm requires two parameters, a minimum cluster size, and the maximum distance allowed between clustered points.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Layer to analyze.

parameters.name: MIN_SIZE
parameters.label: Minimum cluster size
parameters.description: Minimum number of features to generate a cluster.

parameters.name: EPS
parameters.label: Maximum distance between clustered points
parameters.description: Distance beyond which two features can not belongto the same cluster (eps).

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Clusters
parameters.description: Specify the vector layer for the result of the clustering. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

parameters.name: FIELD_NAME
parameters.label: Cluster field name
parameters.description: Name of the field where the associated cluster numbershall be stored.

parameters.name: SIZE_FIELD_NAME

parameters.description: Name of the field with the count of features in the same cluster.

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def dbscan_clustering():
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'MIN_SIZE': 5,
        'EPS': 1.0,
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'DBSCAN*': False,
        'FIELD_NAME': 'CLUSTER_ID',
        'SIZE_FIELD_NAME': 'CLUSTER_SIZE'
    }
    
    result = processing.run('native:dbscanclustering', parameters)
    
    clustered_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Clustered Points', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(clustered_layer)

dbscan_clustering()

# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def delaunay_triangulation():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Points', 'ogr')

    # Run the Delaunay Triangulation algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:delaunaytriangulation', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'TOLERANCE': 0.0,
        'ADD_ATTRIBUTES': True,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })

    # Load the output layer to QGIS
    triangulation_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Delaunay Triangulation', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(triangulation_layer)

# Execute the function
delaunay_triangulation()

brief_description: Takes a polygon layer and removes holes in polygons
full_description:
Takes a polygon layer and removes holes in polygons. It creates a new vector layer in which polygons with holes have been replaced by polygons with only their external ring. Attributes are not modified.
An optional minimum area parameter allows removing only holes which are smaller than a specified area threshold. Leaving this parameter at 0.0 results in all holes being removed.
Allows features in-place modification of polygon features

parameters.label: Cleaned
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

brief_description: Takes a polygon or line layer and generates a new one in which the geometries have a larger number of vertices than the original one.
full_description:
Takes a polygon or line layer and generates a new one in which the geometries have a larger number of vertices than the original one.
The geometries are densified by adding regularly placed extra vertices inside each segment so that the maximum distance between any two vertices does not exceed the specified distance.
If the geometries have Z or M values present then these will be linearly interpolated at the added vertices.
Example
Specifying a distance of 3 would cause the segment [00]->[100] to be converted to [00]->[2.50]->[50]->[7.50]->[100] , since 3 extra vertices are required on the segment and spacing these at 2.5 increments allows them to be evenly spaced over the segment.
Allows features in-place modification of line and polygon features

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input line or polygon vector layer.

parameters.name: INTERVAL
parameters.label: Interval between vertices to add
parameters.description: Maximum distance between two consecutive vertices.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Densified
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer

def detect_vector_changes():
    # Define input and output paths
    original_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    revised_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    unchanged_output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    added_output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    deleted_output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefiles as vector layers
    original_layer = QgsVectorLayer(original_layer_path, 'Original Layer', 'ogr')
    revised_layer = QgsVectorLayer(revised_layer_path, 'Revised Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the detect vector changes algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:detectvectorchanges', {
        'ORIGINAL': original_layer_path,
        'REVISED': revised_layer_path,
        'COMPARE_ATTRIBUTES': [],  # Optional
        'MATCH_TYPE': 1,  # Options: 0 — Exact Match, 1 — Tolerant Match (Topological Equality)
        'UNCHANGED': unchanged_output_path,
        'ADDED': added_output_path,
        'DELETED': deleted_output_path
    })

    # Load the resulting layers to QGIS
    unchanged_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['UNCHANGED'], 'Unchanged Features', 'ogr')
    added_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['ADDED'], 'Added Features', 'ogr')
    deleted_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['DELETED'], 'Deleted Features', 'ogr')
    
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(unchanged_layer)
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(added_layer)
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(deleted_layer)

# Execute the function
detect_vector_changes()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer

def detect_vector_changes():
    # Define input and output paths
    original_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    revised_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    unchanged_output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    added_output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    deleted_output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefiles as vector layers
    original_layer = QgsVectorLayer(original_layer_path, 'Original Layer', 'ogr')
    revised_layer = QgsVectorLayer(revised_layer_path, 'Revised Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the detect vector changes algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:detectvectorchanges', {
        'ORIGINAL': original_layer_path,
        'REVISED': revised_layer_path,
        'COMPARE_ATTRIBUTES': [],  # Optional
        'MATCH_TYPE': 1,  # Options: 0 — Exact Match, 1 — Tolerant Match (Topological Equality)
        'UNCHANGED': unchanged_output_path,
        'ADDED': added_output_path,
        'DELETED': deleted_output_path
    })

    # Load the resulting layers to QGIS
    unchanged_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['UNCHANGED'], 'Unchanged Features', 'ogr')

# Execute the function
detect_vector_changes()

brief_description: Takes a vector layer and combines its features into new features
full_description:
Takes a vector layer and combines its features into new features. One or more attributes can be specified to dissolve features belonging to the same class (having the same value for the specified attributes), alternatively all features can be dissolved to a single feature.
All output geometries will be converted to multi geometries. In case the input is a polygon layer, common boundaries of adjacent polygons being dissolved will get erased. If enabled, the optional “Keep disjoint features separate” setting will cause features and parts that do not overlap or touch to be exported as separate features (instead of parts of a single multipart feature).
The resulting attribute table will have the same fields as the input layer. The values in the output layer’s fields are the ones of the first input feature that happens to be processed.
Default menu: Vector ► Geoprocessing Tools

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input vector layer.

parameters.name: FIELD
parameters.label: Dissolve field(s) Optional
parameters.description: Features having the same value for theselected field(s) will be replaced with asingle one and their geometries are merged. If no field is provided then all thefeatures are dissolved, resulting in asingle (multipart) feature. Fig. 28.80 Dissolve the polygon layer on a common attribute (2 multipart fe

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Dissolved
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

parameters.name: SEPARATE_DISJOINT
parameters.label: Keep disjoint features separate
parameters.description: Parts of dissolved features are exported as separate features(instead of parts of a multipart feature). Fig. 28.81 source (left), dissolve all (3 distinct features - right)  Fig. 28.82 source (left), dissolve on field (5 distinct features - right) .

use_cases: Aggregating parcel data to block or census level
Merging adjacent features with same attribute value
Simplifying boundary datasets for client visualization
Creating regional summaries from local polygons

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def dissolve_layer():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')
    
    # Run the Dissolve algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:dissolve', {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'FIELD': [],  # Optional: provide fields if needed
        'OUTPUT': output_path,  # Options: 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT', 'Save to File…', 'Save to Geopackage…', 'Save to Database Table…'
        'SEPARATE_DISJOINT': False
    })

    # Load the dissolved layer to QGIS
    dissolved_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Dissolved Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(dissolved_layer)

# Execute the function
dissolve_layer()

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def download_vector_tiles(output_path):
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': 'path to your input vector tile layer',  # type [vector tiles]
        'EXTENT': 'user specified extent option',  # options: ['Calculate from layer', 'Calculate from layout map', 'Calculate from bookmark', 'Use map canvas extent', 'Draw on canvas', 'Enter the coordinates as xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax']
        'MAX_ZOOM': 10,  # type [number] default: 10
        'TILE_LIMIT': 100,  # type [number] default: 100
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # type [vector tiles] default: [Save to temporary file]
    }
    result = processing.run("""native:downloadvectortiles", parameters)
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Downloaded Vector Tiles', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

brief_description: NEWin3.34
full_description:
NEWin3.34
Can be used to filter a digital elevation model in order to classify its cells into ground and object (non-ground) cells.
The tool uses concepts as described by Vosselman (2000) and is based on the assumption that a large height difference between two nearby cells is unlikely to be caused by a steep slope in the terrain. The probability that the higher cell might be non-ground increases when the distance between the two cells decreases. Therefore the filter defines a maximum height difference ( dz_max ) between two cells as a function of the distance ( d ) between the cells ( dz_max(d)=d ). A cell is classified as terrain if there is no cell within the kernel radius to which the height difference is larger than the allowed maximum height difference at the distance between these two cells.
The approximate terrain slope ( s ) parameter is used to modify the filter function to match the overall slope in the study area ( dz_max(d)=d*s ). A 5 % confidence interval ( ci=1.65*sqrt(2*stddev) ) may be used to modify the filter function even further by either relaxing ( dz_max(d)=d*s+ci ) or amplifying ( dz_max(d)=d*s-ci ) the filter criterium.
References: Vosselman, G. (2000): Slope based filtering of laser altimetry data. IAPRS, Vol. XXXIII, Part B3, Amsterdam, The Netherlands, 935-942

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Digital Terrain Model raster layer.

parameters.name: BAND
parameters.label: Band number
parameters.description: The band of the DEM to consider.

parameters.name: RADIUS
parameters.label: Kernel radius (pixels)
parameters.description: The radius of the filter kernel (in pixels).Must be large enough to reach ground cells next to non-ground objects..

parameters.name: TERRAIN_SLOPE
parameters.label: Terrain slope (%, pixel size/vertical units)
parameters.description: The approximate terrain slope in % .The terrain slope must be adjusted to account for the ratio of height units vs raster pixel dimensions.Used to relax the filter criterium in steeper terrain..

parameters.name: FILTER_MODIFICATION
parameters.label: Filter modification
parameters.description: Choose whether to apply the filter kernel without modificationor to use a confidence interval to relax or amplify the height criterium. 0 - None 1 - Relax filter 2 - Amplify.

parameters.name: STANDARD_DEVIATION
parameters.label: Standard deviation
parameters.description: The standard deviation used to calculate a 5% confidence interval applied to the height threshold..

parameters.name: OUTPUT_GROUND
parameters.label: Output layer (ground) Optional
parameters.description: Specify the filtered DEM containing only cells classified as ground.One of: Skip Output Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OUTPUT_NONGROUND
parameters.label: Output layer (non-ground objects) Optional
parameters.description: Specify the non-ground objects removed by the filter.One of: Skip Output Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

original_code_example:
# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def dtm_slope_based_filter():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_ground_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    # Load the raster file as a raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_layer_path, 'DEM')
    
    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer_path,
        'BAND': 1,  # Specify the band number
        'RADIUS': 5,  # Kernel radius in pixels
        'TERRAIN_SLOPE': 30,  # Terrain slope in %
        'FILTER_MODIFICATION': 0,  # Options: [0 - None, 1 - Relax filter, 2 - Amplify]
        'STANDARD_DEVIATION': 0.1,
        'OUTPUT_GROUND': output_ground_path,
        'OUTPUT_NONGROUND': 'Skip Output'  # Optional to skip output for non-ground objects
    }
    
    # Run the DTM slope-based filter algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:dtmslopebasedfilter', parameters)
    
    # Load the filtered ground DEM layer to QGIS
    ground_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT_GROUND'], 'Ground Filtered DEM')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(ground_layer)

# Execute the function
dtm_slope_based_filter()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def export_to_dxf(output_path):
    layers_list = ['path/to/first/layer.shp', 'path/to/second/layer.shp']  # Add your input layers to this list
    
    parameters = {
        'LAYERS': layers_list,  # Input vector layers to export
        'SYMBOLOGY_MODE': 0,  # 0—No Symbology, 1—Feature Symbology, 2—Symbol Layer Symbology
        'SYMBOLOGY_SCALE': '1:1 000 000',  # Default scale of data export
        'ENCODING': 'UTF-8',  # Specify encoding for output
        'CRS': 'EPSG:4326',  # Specify CRS for the output layer
        'USE_LAYER_TITLE': False,  # Name the output layer with the layer title instead of the layer name
        'FORCE_2D': False,  # Force 2D
        'MTEXT': False,  # Export labels as MTEXT elements
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Specification of the output DXF file
    }
    
    result = processing.run("""native:dxfexport", parameters)
    
                                                                       
    dxf_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Exported_DXF', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(dxf_layer)

output_path = 'path/to/output'                           
export_to_dxf(output_path)

brief_description: Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the frequency (number of times) the values of an input stack of rasters are equal to the value of a value layer
full_description:
Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the frequency (number of times) the values of an input stack of rasters are equal to the value of a value layer. The output raster extent and resolution are defined by the input raster layer and is always of Int32 type.
If multiband rasters are used in the data raster stack, the algorithm will always perform the analysis on the first band of the rasters - use GDAL to use other bands in the analysis. The output NoData value can be set manually.

parameters.name: INPUT_VALUE_RASTER
parameters.label: Input value raster
parameters.description: The input value layer serves as reference layer for the sample layers.

parameters.name: INPUT_VALUE_RASTER_BAND
parameters.label: Value raster band
parameters.description: Select the band you want to use as sample.
parameters.default: The first band of the raster layer

parameters.name: INPUT_RASTERS
parameters.label: Input raster layers
parameters.description: Raster layers to evaluate.If multiband rasters are used in the data raster stack, the algorithmwill always perform the analysis on the first band of the rasters.

parameters.name: IGNORE_NODATA
parameters.label: Ignore NoData values
parameters.description: If unchecked, any NoData cells in the value raster or the data layer stackwill result in a NoData cell in the output raster.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output layer
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster. One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OUTPUT_NO_DATA_VALUE
parameters.label: Output NoData value Optional
parameters.description: Value to use for NoData in the output layer.

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def equal_to_frequency():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_value_raster = 'path/to/input_file'
    additional_rasters = ['path/to/input_file', 'path/to/input_file']
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    # Load the input raster layer
    input_value_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_value_raster, 'Input Value Raster')
    
    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'INPUT_VALUE_RASTER': input_value_raster,
        'INPUT_VALUE_RASTER_BAND': 1,  # Default is the first band
        'INPUT_RASTERS': additional_rasters,
        'IGNORE_NODATA': False,  # Boolean value to ignore NoData
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OUTPUT_NO_DATA_VALUE': -9999.0  # Default NoData value for output
    }

    # Run the equal to frequency algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:equaltofrequency', parameters)

# Execute the function
equal_to_frequency()

brief_description: Creates a copy of the input layer and adds a new field for every unique key in the HStore field.
full_description:
Creates a copy of the input layer and adds a new field for every unique key in the HStore field.
The expected field list is an optional comma separated list. If this list is specified, only these fields are added and the HStore field is updated. By default, all unique keys are added.
The PostgreSQL HStore is a simple key-value store used in PostgreSQL and GDAL (when reading an OSM file with the other_tags field.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to extract.The HStore field will be updated by removing these keys..

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def explode_lines():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
  
    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Lines', 'ogr')

    # Run the Explode Lines algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:explodelines', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Options: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ), Save to File…, Save to Geopackage…, Save to Database Table…, Append to Layer…
    })

    # Load the exploded lines layer to QGIS
    exploded_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Exploded Lines', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(exploded_layer)

# Execute the function
explode_lines()

def export_mesh_on_grid():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_mesh_path = 'path/to/data'
    output_dir = 'path/to/output'
    output_file_name = """exported_mesh_points.shp"
    output_path = f"{output_dir}/{output_file_name}"

                               
    input_mesh = QgsMeshLayer(input_mesh_path, "Input Mesh Layer", "mesh")

                                                                   
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_mesh_path,
        'DATASET_GROUPS': [],                                 
        'DATASET_TIME': 0,                                                                             
        'EXTENT': None,            
        'GRID_SPACING': '',            
        'CRS_OUTPUT': '',            
        'VECTOR_OPTION': 0,                                                                             
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }

                                           
    result = processing.run("native:exportmeshongrid", parameters)

                                     
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Exported Mesh Points', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
export_mesh_on_grid()

# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def extend_lines(input_layer_path, output_path):
    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_layer_path, 'Input Line Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Extend Lines algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:extendlines', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'START_DISTANCE': 10.0,
        'END_DISTANCE': 15.0,
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Options: Create temporary layer ( 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT' ), Save to File…, Save to Geopackage…, Save to Database Table…, Append to Layer…
    })

    # Load the extended line layer to QGIS
    extended_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Extended Lines', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(extended_layer)

# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsProject
import processing

def extent_to_layer():
    # Define the output path
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Run the Extent to Layer algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:extenttolayer', {
        'INPUT': '0, 1, 0, 1',  # You can replace this with specific coordinates, or a method like Calculate from layer... etc.
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Options: ['TEMPORARY_OUTPUT', 'Save to File...', 'Save to Geopackage...', 'Save to Database Table...']
    })

    # Load the extent layer to QGIS
    extent_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Extent Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(extent_layer)

# Execute the function
extent_to_layer()

            'EXPRESSION':' "STATEFP"  =\'42\' AND  "Obesity" <30',
            'OUTPUT': output_layer_path

        # Perform the extract by attribute operation

        # Load the selected features as a new layer

        QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

brief_description: Extracts label information from a rendered map at a given extent and scale.
full_description:
Extracts label information from a rendered map at a given extent and scale.
If a map theme is provided, the rendered map will match the visibility and symbology of that theme. If left blank, all visible layers from the project will be used. Extracted label information include: position (served as point geometries), the associated layer name and feature ID, label text, rotation (in degree, clockwise), multiline alignment, and font details.

parameters.name: EXTENT
parameters.label: Map extent
parameters.description: Extent of the map to extract the labels from Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout map item in the active project Calculate from bookmark…: uses extent of a saved bookmark Use map canvas

parameters.name: SCALE
parameters.label: Map scale
parameters.description: Extracted labels will be rendered using their properties set at this scale..

parameters.name: MAP_THEME
parameters.label: Map theme Optional
parameters.description: A map theme displaying the layers to extract the labels from.If unset, labels of the currently visible layers are extracted..

parameters.name: INCLUDE_UNPLACED
parameters.label: Include unplaced labels Optional
parameters.description: Specify whether all overlapping labels should be extracted,including the conflicting (thus unplaced) ones..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Extracted labels
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer for the extent(s). One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

brief_description: Takes a vector layer and generates a point layer with points representing specific vertices in the input geometries.
full_description:
Takes a vector layer and generates a point layer with points representing specific vertices in the input geometries.
For instance, this algorithm can be used to extract the first or last vertices in the geometry. The attributes associated to each point are the same ones associated to the feature that the vertex belongs to.
The vertex indices parameter accepts a comma separated string specifying the indices of the vertices to extract. The first vertex corresponds to an index of 0, the second vertex has an index of 1, etc. Negative indices can be used to find vertices at the end of the geometry, e.g., an index of -1 corresponds to the last vertex, -2 corresponds to the second last vertex, etc.
Additional fields are added to the vertices indicating the specific vertex position (e.g., 0, -1, etc), the original vertex index, the vertex’s part and its index within the part (as well as its ring for polygons), distance along the original geometry and bisector angle of vertex for the original geometry.
Allows features in-place modification of point features

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input vector layer.

parameters.name: VERTICES
parameters.label: Vertex indices
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of the indices of the vertices toextract..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Vertices
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def extract_vertices():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the input layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Extract Specific Vertices algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:extractspecificvertices', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'VERTICES': '0',  # Comma-separated string of the indices of the vertices to extract
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })

    # Load the vertices layer to QGIS
    vertices_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Vertices', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(vertices_layer)

# Execute the function
extract_vertices()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject,QgsVectorLayer
def extractwithindistance():
    output_path = "path/to/input_file"
    # Define the parameters Example below:
    parameters = {
        'INPUT':'path/to/input_file', # Input vector layer to copy features from
        'REFERENCE':'path/to/input_file', # Vector layer whose features closeness is used
        'DISTANCE':100, # The maximum distance around reference features to select input features within
        'OUTPUT':output_path
        }
    # Perform the extract by attribute operation
    result = processing.run("native:extractwithindistance", parameters)
    # Load the selected features as a new layer
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Extracted Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)
extractwithindistance()

def fieldcalculator():

    csv_path = "path/to/input_file"
    csv_uri = f"file:///{csv_path}?delimiter=,"

        'INPUT': census_layer,

        'FIELD_TYPE':0, # Available values- 0: Decimal (double), 1: Integer (32 bit), 2: Text (string), 3: Date, 4: Time, 5: Date & Time, 6: Boolean, 7: Binary Object (BLOB), 8: String List, 9: Integer List, 10: Decimal (double) List

        'FORMULA':' ("TPOP2008" - "TPOP2005")/ "TPOP2005" ',

        }
    # Perform the extract by attribute operation
    result = processing.run("native:fieldcalculator", parameters)
    # Load the selected features as a new layer

    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def fill_no_data():
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    input_raster = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, """Input Raster")

    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(filled_raster_layer)

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def filter_vertices_by_z():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Filter Vertices by Z algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:filterverticesbyz', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'MIN': 100,  # Minimum Z value allowed. Set to your required value or remove if not needed
        'MAX': 300,  # Maximum Z value allowed. Set to your required value or remove if not needed
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Options: [Create Temporary Layer (TEMPORARY_OUTPUT), Save to File…, Save to Geopackage…, Save to Database Table…, Append to Layer…]
    })

    # Load the result layer to QGIS
    filtered_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Filtered', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(filtered_layer)

# Execute the function
filter_vertices_by_z()

The gaussian membership function is defined as , where f1 is the spread and f2 the midpoint.

def fuzzify_raster():

    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(fuzzified_layer)

# Execute the function
fuzzify_raster()

brief_description: Transforms an input raster to a fuzzified raster by assigning a membership value to each pixel, using a Linear membership function
full_description: Transforms an input raster to a fuzzified raster by assigning a membership value to each pixel, using a Linear membership function. Membership values range from 0 to 1. In the fuzzified raster, a value of 0 implies no membership of the defined fuzzy set, whereas a value of 1 means full membership. The linear function is defined as , where a is the low bound and b the high bound. This equation assigns membership values using a linear transformation for pixel values between the low and high bounds. Pixels values smaller than the low bound are given 0 membership whereas pixel values greater than the high bound are given 1 membership.

from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsRasterLayer
import processing

def fuzzify_raster_linear():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the raster as a raster layer
    input_raster = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, 'Input Raster')

    # Run the fuzzify raster linear membership algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:fuzzifyrasterlinearmembership', {
        'INPUT': input_raster,
        'BAND': 1,  # Default is the first band
        'FUZZYLOWBOUND': 0,

    fuzzified_raster = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Fuzzified Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(fuzzified_raster)

# Execute the function
fuzzify_raster_linear()

from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def generate_points_pixel_centroids_inside_polygons():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_raster_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    input_vector_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the input layers
    input_raster = QgsRasterLayer(input_raster_path, """Input Raster")
    input_vector = QgsVectorLayer(input_vector_path, "Input Polygon", "ogr")

                               
    parameters = {
        'INPUT_RASTER': input_raster_path,
        'INPUT_VECTOR': input_vector_path,
        'OUTPUT': output_path                                                                                          
    }

                                  
    processing.run("native:generatepointspixelcentroidsinsidepolygons", parameters)

                              
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(output_path, 'Points_Inside_Polygons', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
generate_points_pixel_centroids_inside_polygons()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def geometry_by_expression():
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'OUTPUT_GEOMETRY': 0, # Options: 0 - Polygon, 1 - Line, 2 - Point
        'WITH_Z': False, 
        'WITH_M': False,
        'EXPRESSION': '$geometry',
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }

    result = processing.run('native:geometrybyexpression', parameters)

    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Modified Geometry', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

geometry_by_expression()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer

def convert_gltf_to_vector():
    input_gltf_path = 'path/to/data'
    # Load the input GLTF file
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_gltf_path, """Input GLTF", "3dmodel")

                           
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_gltf_path,
        'OUTPUT_POLYGONS': 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT',                                                                                                                                  
        'OUTPUT_LINES': 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT'                                                                                                                                  
    }

                                           
    result = processing.run("native:gltftovector", parameters)

                                  
    output_polygons_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT_POLYGONS'], 'Polygons', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_polygons_layer)

    output_lines_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT_LINES'], 'Lines', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_lines_layer)

                      
convert_gltf_to_vector()

brief_description: Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the frequency (number of times) the values of an input stack of rasters are equal to the value of a value raster
full_description:
Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the frequency (number of times) the values of an input stack of rasters are equal to the value of a value raster. The output raster extent and resolution is defined by the input raster layer and is always of Int32 type.
If multiband rasters are used in the data raster stack, the algorithm will always perform the analysis on the first band of the rasters - use GDAL to use other bands in the analysis. The output NoData value can be set manually.

parameters.name: INPUT_VALUE_RASTER
parameters.label: Input value raster
parameters.description: The input value layer serves as reference layer for the sample layers.

parameters.name: INPUT_VALUE_RASTER_BAND
parameters.label: Value raster band
parameters.description: Select the band you want to use as sample.
parameters.default: The first band of the raster layer

parameters.name: INPUT_RASTERS
parameters.label: Input raster layers
parameters.description: Raster layers to evaluate.If multiband rasters are used in the data raster stack, the algorithmwill always perform the analysis on the first band of the rasters.

parameters.name: IGNORE_NODATA
parameters.label: Ignore NoData values
parameters.description: If unchecked, any NoData cells in the value raster or the data layer stackwill result in a NoData cell in the output raster.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output layer
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster. One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OUTPUT_NO_DATA_VALUE
parameters.label: Output NoData value Optional
parameters.description: Value to use for NoData in the output layer.

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def greater_than_frequency():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_value_raster_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    input_raster_layers_paths = ['path/to/input_file', 'path/to/input_file']
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    # Load the input value raster as a raster layer
    input_value_raster = QgsRasterLayer(input_value_raster_path, 'Input Value Raster')

    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'INPUT_VALUE_RASTER': input_value_raster_path,
        'INPUT_VALUE_RASTER_BAND': 1,  # The first band of the raster layer
        'INPUT_RASTERS': input_raster_layers_paths,
        'IGNORE_NODATA': False,  # Ignore NoData values. Options: True, False
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OUTPUT_NO_DATA_VALUE': -9999.0  # Default NoData value
    }

    # Perform the greater than frequency operation
    result = processing.run('native:greaterthanfrequency', parameters)

    # Load the output raster layer to QGIS
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Greater Than Frequency Output')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Execute the function
greater_than_frequency()

brief_description: Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the position of the raster with the highest value in a stack of rasters
full_description:
Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the position of the raster with the highest value in a stack of rasters. Position counts start with 1 and range to the total number of input rasters. The order of the input rasters is relevant for the algorithm. If multiple rasters feature the highest value, the first raster will be used for the position value.
If multiband rasters are used in the data raster stack, the algorithm will always perform the analysis on the first band of the rasters - use GDAL to use other bands in the analysis. Any NoData cells in the raster layer stack will result in a NoData cell in the output raster unless the “ignore NoData” parameter is checked. The output NoData value can be set manually. The output rasters extent and resolution is defined by a reference raster layer and is always of Int32 type.

parameters.name: INPUT_RASTERS
parameters.label: Input raster layers
parameters.description: List of raster layers to compare with.

parameters.name: REFERENCE_LAYER
parameters.label: Reference layer
parameters.description: The reference layer for the output layer creation(extent, CRS, pixel dimensions).

parameters.name: IGNORE_NODATA
parameters.label: Ignore NoData values
parameters.description: If unchecked, any NoData cells in the data layer stackwill result in a NoData cell in the output raster.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output layer
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster containing the result.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OUTPUT_NODATA_VALUE
parameters.label: Output NoData value
parameters.description: Value to use for NoData in the output layer.

Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)

        'REFERENCE_LAYER': reference_layer,  # [raster]

        'OUTPUT': output_path,  # [raster] Default: [Save to temporary file]
        'OUTPUT_NODATA_VALUE': -9999.0  # [number] Default: -9999.0
    }
    
    # Run the algorithm
    result = processing.run("""native:highestpositioninrasterstack", parameters)

                                   
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Highest Position in Raster Stack')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
highest_position_in_raster_stack()

        'IGNORE_NODATA': False,  # [boolean] Default: False
        'OUTPUT': output_path,  # [raster] Default: [Save to temporary file]
        'OUTPUT_NODATA_VALUE': -9999.0  # [number] Default: -9999.0
    }
    
    # Run the algorithm
    result = processing.run("""native:highestpositioninrasterstack", parameters)

                                   
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Highest Position in Raster Stack')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
highest_position_in_raster_stack()

brief_description: Creates hub and spoke diagrams by connecting lines from points on the Spoke layer to matching points in the Hub layer.
full_description:
Creates hub and spoke diagrams by connecting lines from points on the Spoke layer to matching points in the Hub layer.
Determination of which hub goes with each point is based on a match between the Hub ID field on the hub points and the Spoke ID field on the spoke points.
If input layers are not point layers, a point on the surface of the geometries will be taken as the connecting location.
Optionally, geodesic lines can be created, which represent the shortest path on the surface of an ellipsoid. When geodesic mode is used, it is possible to split the created lines at the antimeridian (±180 degrees longitude), which can improve rendering of the lines. Additionally, the distance between vertices can be specified. A smaller distance results in a denser, more accurate line.

parameters.name: HUBS
parameters.label: Hub layer
parameters.description: Input layer.

parameters.name: HUB_FIELD
parameters.label: Hub ID field
parameters.description: Field of the hub layer with ID to join.

parameters.name: HUB_FIELDS
parameters.label: Hub layer fields to copy (leave empty to copy all fields) Optional
parameters.description: The field(s) of the hub layer to be copied.If no field(s) are chosen all fields are taken..

parameters.name: SPOKES
parameters.label: Spoke layer
parameters.description: Additional spoke point layer.

parameters.name: SPOKE_FIELD
parameters.label: Spoke ID field
parameters.description: Field of the spoke layer with ID to join.

parameters.name: SPOKE_FIELDS
parameters.label: Spoke layer fields to copy (leave empty to copy all fields) Optional
parameters.description: Field(s) of the spoke layer to be copied.If no fields are chosen all fields are taken..

parameters.name: GEODESIC
parameters.label: Create geodesic lines
parameters.description: Create geodesic lines (the shortest path on the surface ofan ellipsoid).

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Hub lines
parameters.description: Specify the output hub line vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

parameters.name: GEODESIC_DISTANCE
parameters.label: Distance between vertices (geodesic lines only)
parameters.description: Distance between consecutive vertices (in kilometers).A smaller distance results in a denser, more accurate line.

parameters.name: ANTIMERIDIAN_SPLIT
parameters.label: Split lines at antimeridian (±180 degrees longitude)
parameters.description: Split lines at ±180 degrees longitude (to improve renderingof the lines).

use_cases: DEM analysis for site suitability studies
Environmental modeling requiring slope/aspect inputs
Terrain visualization for client deliverables
Erosion risk and geohazard assessment

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def create_hublines():
    hub_layer_path = 'path/to/hub_layer.shp'
    spoke_layer_path = 'path/to/spoke_layer.shp'
    output_path = 'path/to/output_layer.shp'

    hub_layer = QgsVectorLayer(hub_layer_path, 'Hub Layer', 'ogr')
    spoke_layer = QgsVectorLayer(spoke_layer_path, 'Spoke Layer', 'ogr')

    parameters = {
        'HUBS': hub_layer,
        'HUB_FIELD': 'Hub_ID_Field',
        'HUB_FIELDS': ['Field1', 'Field2'],  # or leave empty to copy all fields
        'SPOKES': spoke_layer,
        'SPOKE_FIELD': 'Spoke_ID_Field',
        'SPOKE_FIELDS': ['FieldA', 'FieldB'],  # or leave empty to copy all fields
        'GEODESIC': False,  # True or False
        'OUTPUT': output_path,  # or 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT'
        'GEODESIC_DISTANCE': 1000.0,
        'ANTIMERIDIAN_SPLIT': False  # True or False
    }

    result = processing.run('native:hublines', parameters)
    hublines_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Hub Lines', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(hublines_layer)

create_hublines()

brief_description: Creates a point layer corresponding to the geotagged locations from JPEG or HEIC/HEIF images from a source folder.
full_description:
Creates a point layer corresponding to the geotagged locations from JPEG or HEIC/HEIF images from a source folder.
The point layer will contain a single PointZ feature per input file from which the geotags could be read. Any altitude information from the geotags will be used to set the point’s Z value.
Besides longitude and latitude also altitude, direction and timestamp information, if present in the photo, will be added to the point as attributes.

import processing

def import_geotagged_photos(output_path):
    parameters = {
        'FOLDER': 'path/to/output', # [folder]
        'RECURSIVE': False,  # [boolean] Default: False

        'INVALID': ''  # Optional: Skip Output, Create Temporary Layer, Save to File, Save to Geopackage, Save to Database Table

    result = processing.run("""native:importphotos", parameters)

brief_description: Creates a point geometry interpolated at a set distance along line or curve geometries.
full_description:
Creates a point geometry interpolated at a set distance along line or curve geometries.
Z and M values are linearly interpolated from existing values.
If a multipart geometry is encountered, only the first part is considered when calculating the substring.
If the specified distance is greater than the input feature’s length, the resultant feature will have a null geometry.

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def interpolate_points():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'  # Input layer
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'  # Output path

    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Interpolate Points algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:interpolatepoint', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'DISTANCE': 100.0,  # Distance from the beginning of the line
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })

    # Load the interpolated points layer to QGIS
    interpolated_points_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Interpolated Points', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(interpolated_points_layer)

# Execute the function
interpolate_points()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject,QgsVectorLayer
def intersection():
    output_path = "path/to/input_file"

    # Define the parameters Example below:
    parameters = {
        'INPUT':'path/to/input_file', #Layer to extract (parts of) features from.
        'OVERLAY':'path/to/input_file', # Layer containing the features to check for overlap. Its features’ geometry is expected to have at least as many dimensions (point: 0D, line: 1D, polygon: 2D, volume: 3D) as the input layer’s.
        'INPUT_FIELDS':[], #  Field(s) of the input layer to keep in the output. If no fields are chosen all fields are taken.
        'OVERLAY_FIELDS':[], # Field(s) of the overlay layer to keep in the output. If no fields are chosen all fields are taken. Duplicate field names will be appended a count suffix to avoid collision.
        'OVERLAY_FIELDS_PREFIX':'',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'GRID_SIZE':None}
    # Perform the extract by attribute operation
    result = processing.run("native:intersection", parameters)
    # Load the selected features as a new layer
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Intersection', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)
intersection()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject,QgsVectorLayer
def joinattributesbylocation():
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Define the parameters Example below:
    parameters = {
        'INPUT':'path/to/input_file',
        'PREDICATE':[5], #Available values - 0: intersect, 1: contain, 2: equal, 3: touch, 4: overlap, 5: are within, 6: cross
        'JOIN':'path/to/input_file',
        'JOIN_FIELDS':[], # Available values- 0: Create separate feature for each matching feature (one-to-many), 1: Take attributes of the first matching feature only (one-to-one), 2: Take attributes of the feature with largest overlap only (one-to-one)
        'METHOD':0,
        'DISCARD_NONMATCHING':True,
        'PREFIX':'',
        'OUTPUT': output_path
        }
    # Perform the extract by attribute operation
    result = processing.run("native:joinattributesbylocation", parameters)
    # Load the selected features as a new layer
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Joined attributes, 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)
joinattributesbylocation()

brief_description: Takes an input vector layer and creates a new vector layer with additional fields in its attribute table
full_description:
Takes an input vector layer and creates a new vector layer with additional fields in its attribute table. The additional attributes and their values are taken from a second vector layer. Features are joined by finding the closest features from each layer.
By default only the nearest feature is joined, but the join can also join to the k-nearest neighboring features.
If a maximum distance is specified, only features which are closer than this distance will be matched.

parameters.description: Join layer fields to copy (if empty, all fields will becopied)..

parameters.name: DISCARD_NONMATCHING

parameters.description: Remove from the output the input layer records which could notbe joined.

parameters.name: PREFIX
parameters.label: Joined field prefix
parameters.description: Joined field prefix.

parameters.name: NEIGHBORS
parameters.label: Maximum nearest neighbors
parameters.description: Maximum number of nearest neighbors.

parameters.name: MAX_DISTANCE
parameters.label: Maximum distance
parameters.description: Maximum search distance.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Joined layer Optional
parameters.description: Specify the vector layer containing the joined features.One of: Skip Output Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def join_by_nearest():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    input_2_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    # Load the input layers
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')
    input_2_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_2_path, 'Input Layer 2', 'ogr')
    
    # Run the Join by Nearest algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:joinbynearest', {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'INPUT_2': input_2_path,
        'FIELDS_TO_COPY': [],  # Leave empty to copy all fields
        'DISCARD_NONMATCHING': False,  # Default: False
        'PREFIX': 'joined_',
        'NEIGHBORS': 1,  # Default value
        'MAX_DISTANCE': 1000,  # Example max distance
        'OUTPUT': output_path,  # Output path
        'NON_MATCHING': 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT'  # Default: [Skip output]
    })
    
    # Load the joined layer to QGIS
    joined_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Joined Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(joined_layer)

# Execute the function
join_by_nearest()

brief_description: Calculates the 2D distance based k-means cluster number for each input feature.
full_description:
Calculates the 2D distance based k-means cluster number for each input feature.
K-means clustering aims to partition the features into k clusters in which each feature belongs to the cluster with the nearest mean. The mean point is represented by the barycenter of the clustered features.
If input geometries are lines or polygons, the clustering is based on the centroid of the feature.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Layer to analyze.

parameters.name: CLUSTERS
parameters.label: Number of clusters
parameters.description: Number of clusters to create with the features.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Clusters
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer for generated the clusters.One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

parameters.name: FIELD_NAME
parameters.label: Cluster field name
parameters.description: Name of the field where the associated cluster numbershall be stored.

parameters.name: SIZE_FIELD_NAME
parameters.label: Cluster size field name
parameters.description: Name of the field with the count of features in the same cluster.

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def generate_clusters():
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')
    
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'CLUSTERS': 5,
        'OUTPUT': output_path,  # 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT', 'memory:' or other options could be used
        'FIELD_NAME': 'CLUSTER_ID',
        'SIZE_FIELD_NAME': 'CLUSTER_SIZE'
    }
    
    result = processing.run('native:kmeansclustering', parameters)
    
    clustered_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Clusters', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(clustered_layer)

generate_clusters()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def convert_layer_to_bookmarks():
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')
    
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'DESTINATION': 0,  # 0 — Project bookmarks, 1 — User bookmarks
        'NAME_EXPRESSION': 'name_field',
        'GROUP_EXPRESSION': 'group_field'
    }
    
    result = processing.run('native:layertobookmarks', parameters)
    
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(output_path, 'Bookmarks Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

convert_layer_to_bookmarks()

brief_description: Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the frequency (number of times) the values of an input stack of rasters are less than the value of a value raster
full_description:
Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the frequency (number of times) the values of an input stack of rasters are less than the value of a value raster. The output raster extent and resolution is defined by the input raster layer and is always of Int32 type.
If multiband rasters are used in the data raster stack, the algorithm will always perform the analysis on the first band of the rasters - use GDAL to use other bands in the analysis. The output NoData value can be set manually.

parameters.name: INPUT_VALUE_RASTER
parameters.label: Input value raster
parameters.description: The input value layer serves as reference layer for the sample layers.

parameters.name: INPUT_VALUE_RASTER_BAND
parameters.label: Value raster band
parameters.description: Select the band you want to use as sample.
parameters.default: The first band of the raster layer

parameters.name: INPUT_RASTERS
parameters.label: Input raster layers
parameters.description: Raster layers to evaluate.If multiband rasters are used in the data raster stack, the algorithmwill always perform the analysis on the first band of the rasters.

parameters.name: IGNORE_NODATA
parameters.label: Ignore NoData values
parameters.description: If unchecked, any NoData cells in the value raster or the data layer stackwill result in a NoData cell in the output raster.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output layer
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster. One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OUTPUT_NO_DATA_VALUE
parameters.label: Output NoData value Optional
parameters.description: Value to use for NoData in the output layer.

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def lessthanfrequency_algorithm():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_value_raster_path = 'path/to/input_value_raster.tif'
    input_rasters_paths = ['path/to/input_raster1.tif', 'path/to/input_raster2.tif']
    output_path = 'path/to/output_raster.tif'

    # Load the raster layers
    input_value_raster = QgsRasterLayer(input_value_raster_path, 'Input Value Raster')
    input_rasters = [QgsRasterLayer(path, f'Input Raster {i+1}') for i, path in enumerate(input_rasters_paths)]

    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'INPUT_VALUE_RASTER': input_value_raster,
        'INPUT_VALUE_RASTER_BAND': 1,  # Default: The first band of the raster layer
        'INPUT_RASTERS': input_rasters,
        'IGNORE_NODATA': False,  # If unchecked, any NoData cells result in a NoData cell in the output raster
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OUTPUT_NO_DATA_VALUE': -9999.0,  # Optional. Default: -9999.0
    }

    # Run the lessthanfrequency algorithm
    result = processing.run("""native:lessthanfrequency", parameters)
    
                                   
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'LessThanFrequency Output')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

                      
lessthanfrequency_algorithm()

brief_description: Calculates for each raster cell, the density measure of linear features within a circular neighbourhood
full_description: Calculates for each raster cell, the density measure of linear features within a circular neighbourhood. This measure is obtained by summing all the line segments intersecting the circular neighbourhood and dividing this sum by the area of such neighbourhood. A weighting factor can be applied to the line segments.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input line layer
parameters.description: Input vector layer containing line features.

parameters.name: WEIGHT
parameters.label: Weight field
parameters.description: Field of the layer containing the weight factor to use duringthe calculation.

parameters.name: RADIUS
parameters.label: Search Radius
parameters.description: Radius of the circular neighbourhood. Units can be specifiedhere..

parameters.name: PIXEL_SIZE
parameters.label: Pixel size
parameters.description: Pixel size of the output raster layer in layer units.The raster has square pixels..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Line density raster
parameters.description: The output as a raster layer. One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def calculate_line_density():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the input line layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Line Layer', 'ogr')

    # Define the parameters for the line density calculation
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'WEIGHT': 'weight_field',  # specify the name of the weight field in the layer
        'RADIUS': 10,
        'PIXEL_SIZE': 10,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }

    # Run the Line Density algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:linedensity', parameters)

    # Load the output raster layer to QGIS
    line_density_layer = QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(result['OUTPUT'])

# Execute the function
calculate_line_density()

brief_description: Returns the portion of a line (or curve) which falls between the specified start and end distances (measured from the beginning of the line).
full_description:
Returns the portion of a line (or curve) which falls between the specified start and end distances (measured from the beginning of the line).
Z and M values are linearly interpolated from existing values.
If a multipart geometry is encountered, only the first part is considered when calculating the substring.
Allows features in-place modification of line features

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input line vector layer.

parameters.name: START_DISTANCE
parameters.label: Start distance
parameters.description: Distance along the input line to the start point ofthe output feature.

parameters.name: END_DISTANCE
parameters.label: End distance
parameters.description: Distance along the input line to the end point ofthe output feature.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Substring
parameters.description: Specify the output line vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

brief_description: Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the position of the raster with the lowest value in a stack of rasters
full_description:
Evaluates on a cell-by-cell basis the position of the raster with the lowest value in a stack of rasters. Position counts start with 1 and range to the total number of input rasters. The order of the input rasters is relevant for the algorithm. If multiple rasters feature the lowest value, the first raster will be used for the position value.
If multiband rasters are used in the data raster stack, the algorithm will always perform the analysis on the first band of the rasters - use GDAL to use other bands in the analysis. Any NoData cells in the raster layer stack will result in a NoData cell in the output raster unless the “ignore NoData” parameter is checked. The output NoData value can be set manually. The output rasters extent and resolution is defined by a reference raster layer and is always of Int32 type.

parameters.name: INPUT_RASTERS
parameters.label: Input raster layers
parameters.description: List of raster layers to compare with.

parameters.name: REFERENCE_LAYER
parameters.label: Reference layer
parameters.description: The reference layer for the output layer creation(extent, CRS, pixel dimensions).

parameters.name: IGNORE_NODATA
parameters.label: Ignore NoData values
parameters.description: If unchecked, any NoData cells in the data layer stackwill result in a NoData cell in the output raster.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output layer
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster containing the result.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: OUTPUT_NODATA_VALUE
parameters.label: Output NoData value
parameters.description: Value to use for NoData in the output layer.

brief_description: Computes a point layer with the center of mass of geometries in an input layer.
full_description:
Computes a point layer with the center of mass of geometries in an input layer.
An attribute can be specified as containing weights to be applied to each feature when computing the center of mass.
If an attribute is selected in the parameter, features will be grouped according to values in this field. Instead of a single point with the center of mass of the whole layer, the output layer will contain a center of mass for the features in each category.
Default menu: Vector ► Analysis Tools

Unique 
ID field

parameters.description: Specify the (point vector) layer for the result. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

brief_description: Combines multiple vector layers of the same geometry type into a single one.
full_description:
Combines multiple vector layers of the same geometry type into a single one.
The attribute table of the resulting layer will contain the fields from all input layers. If fields with the same name but different types are found then the exported field will be automatically converted into a string type field. New fields storing the original layer name and source are also added.
If any input layers contain Z or M values, then the output layer will also contain these values. Similarly, if any of the input layers are multi-part, the output layer will also be a multi-part layer.
Optionally, the destination coordinate reference system (CRS) for the merged layer can be set. If it is not set, the CRS will be taken from the first input layer. All layers will be reprojected to match this CRS.
Default menu: Vector ► Data Management Tools

parameters.name: LAYERS
parameters.label: Input Layers
parameters.description: The layers that are to be merged into asingle layer.Layers should be of the same geometry type..

parameters.name: CRS
parameters.label: Destination CRS Optional
parameters.description: Choose the CRS for the output layer.If not specified, the CRS of the first inputlayer is used..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Merged
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def merge_vector_layers():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_layer_paths = ['path/to/input_file', 'path/to/input_file']
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefiles as vector layers
    input_layers = [QgsVectorLayer(path, f'Layer {i+1}', 'ogr') for i, path in enumerate(input_layer_paths)]

    # Run the Merge Vector Layers algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:mergevectorlayers', {
        'LAYERS': input_layers, # List of layers to be merged
        'CRS': 'EPSG:4326',  # Optional; specify CRS if different from the first layer. If none, remove this line
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Specify the output path
    })

    # Load the merged layer to QGIS
    merged_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Merged Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(merged_layer)

# Execute the function
merge_vector_layers()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsMeshLayer, QgsProject

def export_mesh_contours():
    input_mesh_layer = 'path/to/mesh_layer'  # Define the path to the input mesh layer
    output_lines_path = 'path/to/output_lines.shp'  # Output path for contour lines
    output_polygons_path = 'path/to/output_polygons.shp'  # Output path for contour polygons
    
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_mesh_layer,
        'DATASET_GROUPS': [],  # Type: [layer] [list]
        'DATASET_TIME': 1,  # Options: 0: 'Current canvas time', 1: 'Defined date/time', 2: 'Dataset group time step'
        'INCREMENT': None,  
        'MINIMUM': None,  
        'MAXIMUM': None,  
        'CONTOUR_LEVEL_LIST': None,  
        'CRS_OUTPUT': None,  
        'OUTPUT_LINES': output_lines_path,
        'OUTPUT_POLYGONS': output_polygons_path
    }
    
    result = processing.run("""native:meshcontours", parameters)
    
    output_lines_layer = QgsMeshLayer(result['OUTPUT_LINES'], 'Contour_Lines', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_lines_layer)
    
    output_polygons_layer = QgsMeshLayer(result['OUTPUT_POLYGONS'], 'Contour_Polygons', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_polygons_layer)

export_mesh_contours()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsMeshLayer, QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def export_mesh_cross_section():
    input_mesh_path = 'path/to/data'
    dataset_groups = []  # define your dataset groups here
    dataset_time = None  # set to 0, 1, or 2 as needed
    input_lines_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    input_mesh = QgsMeshLayer(input_mesh_path, """Input Mesh Layer", "ogr")
    input_lines = QgsVectorLayer(input_lines_path, "Input Lines Layer", "ogr")
    
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_mesh,
        'DATASET_GROUPS': dataset_groups,
        'DATASET_TIME': dataset_time,                                                                                        
        'INPUT_LINES': input_lines,
        'RESOLUTION': 10.0,
        'DATASET_DIGITS': 2,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }
    
    result = processing.run("native:meshexportcrosssection", parameters)
    
export_mesh_cross_section()
