brief_description: Creates a raster layer from a mesh dataset.
full_description:
Creates a raster layer from a mesh dataset.
For data on volume (3D stacked dataset values), the exported dataset values are averaged on faces using the method defined in the mesh layer properties (default is Multi level averaging method). 1D meshes are not supported.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input mesh layer
parameters.description: The mesh layer to export data from.

parameters.name: DATASET_GROUPS
parameters.label: Dataset groups
parameters.description: The dataset groups.

parameters.name: DATASET_TIME
parameters.label: Dataset time
parameters.description: The time range to take into account 0 — Current canvas time 1 — Defined date/time 2 — Dataset group time step.

parameters.name: EXTENT
parameters.label: Extent Optional
parameters.description: Specify the spatial extent on which to process the data. Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout map item in the active project Calculate from bookmark…: uses extent of a saved bookmark Use

parameters.name: PIXEL_SIZE
parameters.label: Pixel size
parameters.description: Pixel size of the output raster layer..

parameters.name: CRS_OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output coordinate system Optional
parameters.description: Coordinate Reference System to assign to the output.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output raster layer
parameters.description: Specification of the output file. One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems
original_code_example:
import processing
from qgis.core import QgsMeshLayer, QgsProject, QgsCoordinateReferenceSystem

def mesh_rasterize():
    mesh_layer_path = 'path/to/data'  # input mesh layer
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'  # output file path
    
    mesh_layer = QgsMeshLayer(mesh_layer_path, 'Input Mesh', 'mdal')

    parameters = {
        'INPUT': mesh_layer_path,
        'DATASET_GROUPS': '1',  # Dataset group index or name
        'DATASET_TIME': '0',  # 0: Current canvas time, 1: Defined date/time, 2: Dataset group time step
        'EXTENT': None,  # Specify extent here if needed
        'PIXEL_SIZE': 1.0,
        'CRS_OUTPUT': QgsCoordinateReferenceSystem(),  # Specify CRS if needed, else leave empty
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }

    result = processing.run("""native:meshrasterize", parameters)

    raster_layer = QgsProject.instance().mapLayersByName('Output Raster')[0]
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(raster_layer)

mesh_rasterize()

original_code_example:
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def minimum_enclosing_circle():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Minimum Enclosing Circle algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:minimumenclosingcircle', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'SEGMENTS': 72,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })

    # Load the resulting layer to QGIS
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Minimum Enclosing Circles', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Execute the function
minimum_enclosing_circle()

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def multipart_to_singleparts():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Multipart Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Multipart to Singleparts algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:multiparttosingleparts', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # options: Create Temporary Layer (TEMPORARY_OUTPUT), Save to...
    })

    # Load the single parts layer to QGIS
    singleparts_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Single Parts', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(singleparts_layer)

# Execute the function
multipart_to_singleparts()

def perform_nearest_neighbour_analysis():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/output'
  
    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer

        'INPUT': input_path,  # Point vector layer

# Execute the function

brief_description: Offsets lines by a specified distance
full_description:
Offsets lines by a specified distance. Positive distances will offset lines to the left, and negative distances will offset them to the right.
Allows features in-place modification of line features

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input line vector layer.

parameters.name: DISTANCE
parameters.label: Distance
parameters.description: Offset distance.You can use the Data Defined button on the right to choosea field from which the radius will be calculated.This way you can have different radius for each feature(see Variable distance buffer )..

parameters.name: SEGMENTS
parameters.label: Segments
parameters.description: Controls the number of line segments to use to approximatea quarter circle when creating rounded offsets..

parameters.name: JOIN_STYLE
parameters.label: Join style
parameters.description: Specifies whether round, miter or beveled joins should beused when offsetting corners in a line.Options are: 0 — Round 1 — Miter 2 — Bevel Fig. 28.96 Round, miter, and bevel join styles .

parameters.name: MITER_LIMIT
parameters.label: Miter limit
parameters.description: Sets the maximum distance from the offset geometry to usewhen creating a mitered join as a factor of the offset distance (only applicable for miterjoin styles).Minimum: 1.0 Fig. 28.97 A 10m buffer with a limit of 2 and a 10m buffer with a limit of 1 .

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description: Specify the output (offset) layer.One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def offset_line():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Lines', 'ogr')

    # Run the Offset Line algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:offsetline', {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'DISTANCE': 10.0,
        'SEGMENTS': 8,
        'JOIN_STYLE': 0,  # 0 — Round, 1 — Miter, 2 — Bevel
        'MITER_LIMIT': 2.0,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })

    # Load the offset line layer to QGIS
    offset_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Offset Lines', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(offset_layer)

# Execute the function
offset_line()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer

def ordered_by_expression():
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'EXPRESSION': 'field_name',  # replace with the actual field name
        'ASCENDING': True,  # if False, will sort from large to small values
        'NULLS_FIRST': False,  # if True, will place null values first
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # output options: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table…
    }

    result = processing.run('native:orderbyexpression', parameters)

    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Ordered Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

ordered_by_expression()

brief_description: Attempts to orthogonalize the geometries of the input line or polygon layer
full_description:
Attempts to orthogonalize the geometries of the input line or polygon layer. This process shifts the vertices in the geometries to try to make every angle in the geometry either a right angle or a straight line.
Allows features in-place modification of line and polygon features

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input line or polygon vector layer.

parameters.name: ANGLE_TOLERANCE
parameters.label: Maximum angle tolerance (degrees)
parameters.description: Specify the maximum deviation from a right angle or straightline a vertex can have for it to be adjusted.Smaller tolerances mean that only vertices which are alreadycloser to right angles will be adjusted, and larger tolerancesmean that vertices which deviate further from right angles willalso be ad

parameters.name: MAX_ITERATIONS
parameters.label: Maximum algorithm iterations
parameters.description: Setting a larger number for the maximum number of iterationswill result in a more orthogonal geometry at the cost of extraprocessing time..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Orthogonalized
parameters.description: Specify the output polygon vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

Terrain visualization for client deliverables

original_code_example:
# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def orthogonalize_geometry():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')
    
    # Run the Orthogonalize algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:orthogonalize', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'ANGLE_TOLERANCE': 15,
        'MAX_ITERATIONS': 1000,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })
    
    # Load the orthogonalized layer to QGIS
    orthogonalized_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Orthogonalized Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(orthogonalized_layer)

# Execute the function
orthogonalize_geometry()
```

brief_description: Adds layers to a GeoPackage.
full_description:
Adds layers to a GeoPackage.
If the GeoPackage exists and OverwriteexistingGeoPackage is checked, it will be overwritten (removed and recreated). If the GeoPackage exists and OverwriteexistingGeoPackage is not checked, the layer will be appended.

parameters.name: LAYERS
parameters.label: Input layers
parameters.description: The (vector) layers to import into the GeoPackage.Raster layers are not supported. If a raster layer isadded, a QgsProcessingException will be thrown..

parameters.name: OVERWRITE
parameters.label: Overwrite existing GeoPackage
parameters.description: If the specified GeoPackage exists, setting this option to True will make sure that it is deleted and a new onewill be created before the layers are added.If set to False , layers will be appended..

parameters.name: SAVE_STYLES
parameters.label: Save layer styles into GeoPackage
parameters.description: Save the layer styles.

parameters.name: SELECTED_FEATURES_ONLY
parameters.label: Save only selected features
parameters.description: If a layer has a selection, setting this option to True will result in only selected features being saved. Forlayers without a selection all features will be saved..

parameters.name: EXPORT_RELATED_LAYERS
parameters.label: Export related layers following relations defined in the project
parameters.description: If an input layer has relations set in the project,setting this option to True will result in exporting also its related layer(s).If the layer has features selected, then only their related features will be exportedunless the related layer was also an input layer..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Destination GeoPackage
parameters.description: Specify where to store the GeoPackage file. One of Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def export_to_geopackage():
    # Define the input layers
    input_layers = ['path/to/layer1.shp', 'path/to/layer2.shp']  # list of paths to vector layers

    # Output path for GeoPackage
    output_path = 'path/to/output.gpkg'

    # Define processing parameters
    parameters = {
        'LAYERS': input_layers,
        'OVERWRITE': False,
        'SAVE_STYLES': True,
        'SELECTED_FEATURES_ONLY': False,
        'EXPORT_RELATED_LAYERS': False,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }

    # Invoke the processing tool
    result = processing.run("""native:package", parameters)

                                              
    output_gpkg_layer = QgsVectorLayer(output_path, 'GeoPackage_output', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_gpkg_layer)

                      
export_to_geopackage()

brief_description: Converts a point layer to a line layer, by joining points in an order defined by an expression or a field in the input point layer.
full_description:
Converts a point layer to a line layer, by joining points in an order defined by an expression or a field in the input point layer.
Points can be grouped by a field or an expression to distinguish line features.
In addition to the line vector layer, a text file is output that describes the resulting line as a start point and a sequence of bearings / directions (relative to azimuth) and distances.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input point layer
parameters.description: Input point vector layer.

parameters.name: CLOSE_PATH
parameters.label: Create closed paths
parameters.description: If checked, the first and last points of the line will be connectedand close the generated path.

parameters.name: ORDER_EXPRESSION
parameters.label: Order expression Optional
parameters.description: Field or expression providing the order to connect the points in the path.If not set, the feature ID ( $id ) is used..

parameters.name: NATURAL_SORT
parameters.label: Sort text containing numbers naturally Optional
parameters.description: If checked, naturally sorts the features based on the provided expression(i.e., ‘a9’ < ‘a10’)..

parameters.name: GROUP_EXPRESSION
parameters.label: Path group expression Optional
parameters.description: Point features of the same value in the field or expression will begrouped in the same line.If not set, a single path is drawn with all the input points..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Paths
parameters.description: Specify the line vector layer of the path. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

parameters.name: OUTPUT_TEXT_DIR
parameters.label: Directory for text output Optional
parameters.description: Specify the directory that will contain the descriptionfiles of points and paths. One of: Skip Output Save to a Temporary Directory Save to Directory.

from qgis.core import QgsPointXY, QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer
import processing

def point_to_layer():
    # Define the input point with CRS info
    input_point = QgsPointXY(397254, 6214446)
    crs_projection = 'EPSG:32632'
    output_path = 'memory:'

    # Set parameters
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': {'type': 'Point', 'coordinates': input_point, 'crs': crs_projection},
        'OUTPUT': 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT'  # Create Temporary Layer
    }
    
    # Perform the point to layer operation
    result = processing.run('native:pointtolayer', parameters)
    
    # Load the output layer to QGIS
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Output_Point_Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Execute the function
point_to_layer()

brief_description: Calculates the pole of inaccessibility for a polygon layer, which is the most distant internal point from the boundary of the surface.
full_description:
Calculates the pole of inaccessibility for a polygon layer, which is the most distant internal point from the boundary of the surface.
This algorithm uses the ‘polylabel’ algorithm (Vladimir Agafonkin, 2016), which is an iterative approach guaranteed to find the true pole of inaccessibility within a specified tolerance. A more precise tolerance (lower value) requires more iterations and will take longer to calculate.
The distance from the calculated pole to the polygon boundary will be stored as a new attribute in the output layer.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input vector layer.

parameters.name: TOLERANCE
parameters.label: Tolerance
parameters.description: Set the tolerance for the calculation.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Point
parameters.description: Specify the output polygon vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def calculate_pole_of_inaccessibility():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_layer_path, 'Polygon Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Pole of Inaccessibility algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:poleofinaccessibility', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'TOLERANCE': 1.0,
        'OUTPUT': output_layer_path  # Create Temporary Layer, Save to File…, Save to Geopackage…, Save to Database Table…, Append to Layer…
    })

    # Load the result layer to QGIS
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Pole of Inaccessibility', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Execute the function
calculate_pole_of_inaccessibility()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer

def project_points():
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
  
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Points', 'ogr')
    
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'BEARING': 45.0,
        'DISTANCE': 5.0,
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Options: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer…
    }
    
    result = processing.run('native:projectpointcartesian', parameters)
    
    projected_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Projected Points', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(projected_layer)

project_points()

A distance factor can be specified, to avoid points being too close to each other. If the minimum distance between points makes it impossible to create new points, either distance can be decreased or the maximum number of attempts may be increased.
Default menu: Vector ► Research Tools

parameters.name: POINTS_NUMBER

parameters.description: Number of point to create.

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsCoordinateReferenceSystem

def create_random_points():
    # Define the output path
    output_path = """path/to/input_file"
    
    target_crs = QgsCoordinateReferenceSystem("EPSG:4326")                                                  
    
                                      
    parameters = {
        'EXTENT': '0,10,0,10',                        
        'POINTS_NUMBER': 100,                           
        'MIN_DISTANCE': 0.0,
        'TARGET_CRS': target_crs,
        'OUTPUT': output_path, 
        'MAX_ATTEMPTS': 200
    }
    
                           
    result = processing.run("native:randompointsinextent", parameters)
    
                              
    random_points_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Random Points', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(random_points_layer)

create_random_points()

brief_description: Creates a point layer with points placed inside the polygons of another layer.
full_description:
Creates a point layer with points placed inside the polygons of another layer.
For each feature (polygon / multi-polygon) geometry in the input layer, the given number of points is added to the result layer.
Per feature and global minimum distances can be specified in order to avoid points being too close in the output point layer. If a minimum distance is specified, it may not be possible to generate the specified number of points for each feature. The total number of generated points and missed points are available as output from the algorithm.
The illustration below shows the effect of per feature and global minimum distances and zero/non-zero minimum distances (generated with the same seed, so at least the first point generated will be the same).
The maximum number of tries per point can be specified. This is only relevant for non-zero minimum distance.
A seed for the random number generator can be provided, making it possible to get identical random number sequences for different runs of the algorithm.
The attributes of the polygon feature on which a point was generated can be included (Include polygon attributes).
If you want approximately the same point density for all the features, you can data-define the number of points using the area of the polygon feature geometry.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input polygon layer
parameters.description: Input polygon vector layer.

parameters.name: POINTS_NUMBER
parameters.label: Number of points for each feature
parameters.description: Number of points to create.

parameters.name: MIN_DISTANCE
parameters.label: Minimum distance between points Optional
parameters.description: The minimum distance between points within one polygon feature.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Random points in polygons
parameters.description: The output random points. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

parameters.name: MIN_DISTANCE_GLOBAL
parameters.label: Global minimum distance between points Optional
parameters.description: The global minimum distance between points.Should be smaller than the Minimum distance betweenpoints (per feature) for that parameter to have an effect..

parameters.name: MAX_TRIES_PER_POINT
parameters.label: Maximum number of search attempts (for Min
parameters.description: dist. > 0) Optional. The maximum number of tries per point.Only relevant if the minimum distance between points is set(and greater than 0)..

parameters.name: SEED
parameters.label: Random seed Optional
parameters.description: The seed to use for the random number generator..

parameters.name: INCLUDE_POLYGON_ATTRIBUTES
parameters.label: Include polygon attributes
parameters.description: If set, a point will get the attributes from the line onwhich it is placed..

Environmental modeling requiring slope/aspect inputs
Terrain visualization for client deliverables
Erosion risk and geohazard assessment

brief_description: Calculates the boolean AND for a set of input rasters
full_description:
Calculates the boolean AND for a set of input rasters. If all of the input rasters have a non-zero value for a pixel, that pixel will be set to 1 in the output raster. If any of the input rasters have 0 values for the pixel it will be set to 0 in the output raster.
The reference layer parameter specifies an existing raster layer to use as a reference when creating the output raster. The output raster will have the same extent, CRS, and pixel dimensions as this layer.
By default, a NoData pixel in ANY of the input layers will result in a NoData pixel in the output raster. If the Treat NoData values as false option is checked, then NoData inputs will be treated the same as a 0 input value.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layers
parameters.description: List of input raster layers.

parameters.name: REF_LAYER
parameters.label: Reference layer
parameters.description: The reference layer to create the output layerfrom (extent, CRS, pixel dimensions).

parameters.name: NODATA_AS_FALSE
parameters.label: Treat NoData values as false
parameters.description: Treat NoData values in the input files as 0 when performing theoperation.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output layer
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster containing the result.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: NO_DATA
parameters.label: Output NoData value
parameters.description: Value to use for NoData in the output layer.

parameters.name: DATA_TYPE
parameters.label: Output data type
parameters.description: Output raster data type. Options: 0 — Byte (Eight bit unsigned integer (quint8)) 1 — Int16 (Sixteen bit signed integer (qint16)) 2 — UInt16 (Sixteen bit unsigned integer (quint16)) 3 — Int32 (Thirty two bit signed integer (qint32)) 4 — UInt32 (Thirty two bit unsigned integer (quint32)) 5 — Float32 (

def raster_boolean_and(input_paths, ref_layer_path, output_path):

        'INPUT': input_paths,
        'REF_LAYER': ref_layer_path,
        'NODATA_AS_FALSE': False,
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'NO_DATA': -9999.0,
        'DATA_TYPE': 5  # Options: 0 — Byte, 1 — Int16, 2 — UInt16, 3 — Int32, 4 — UInt32, 5 — Float32, 6 — Float64, 7 — CInt16, 8 — CInt32, 9 — CFloat32, 10 — CFloat64, 11 — Int8 
    }

    result = processing.run('native:rasterbooleanand', parameters)
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Boolean AND Result')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

brief_description: Creates a raster image of map canvas content.
full_description:
Creates a raster image of map canvas content.
A map theme can be selected to render a predetermined set of layers with a defined style for each layer.
Alternatively, a single layer can be selected if no map theme is set.
If neither map theme nor layer is set, the current map content will be rendered. The minimum extent entered will internally be extended to be a multiple of the tile size.

parameters.name: EXTENT
parameters.label: Minimum extent to render (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax)
parameters.description: Specify the extent of the output raster layer.It will internally be extended to a multiple of the tile size. Available methods are: Calculate from layer…: uses extent of a layer loaded in the current project Calculate from layout map…: uses extent of a layout map item in the active project Calculate

parameters.name: TILE_SIZE
parameters.label: Tile size
parameters.description: Size of the tile of the output raster layer. Minimum value: 64..

parameters.name: MAP_UNITS_PER_PIXEL
parameters.label: Map units per pixel
parameters.description: Pixel size (in map units). Minimum value: 0.0.

parameters.name: MAKE_BACKGROUND_TRANSPARENT
parameters.label: Make background transparent
parameters.description: Allows exporting the map with a transparent background.Outputs an RGBA (instead of RGB) image if set to True ..

parameters.name: MAP_THEME
parameters.label: Map theme to render Optional
parameters.description: Use an existing map theme for therendering..

parameters.name: LAYER

brief_description: Returns basic properties of the given raster layer, including the extent, size in pixels and dimensions of pixels (in map units), number of bands, and NoData value.
full_description:
Returns basic properties of the given raster layer, including the extent, size in pixels and dimensions of pixels (in map units), number of bands, and NoData value.
This algorithm is intended for use as a means of extracting these useful properties to use as the input values to other algorithms in a model - e.g. to allow to pass an existing raster’s pixel sizes over to a GDAL raster algorithm.

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject

def rasterlayerproperties():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the raster file
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, 'Input Raster')

    # Run raster layer properties algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:rasterlayerproperties', {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'BAND': 1,  # Specify band number. Set to None if not specifying a band
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })

    # Output not loaded into QGIS as it's a properties text file
    print(f"""Raster properties saved to {output_path}")

def raster_layer_zonal_stats():

    zones_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    result = processing.run("""native:rasterlayerzonalstats", parameters)

    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer, QgsRasterLayer

def raster_sampling():
    input_vector_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    input_raster_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    input_vector_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_vector_path, 'Input Points', 'ogr')
    input_raster_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_raster_path, 'Input Raster')
    
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_vector_path,
        'RASTERCOPY': input_raster_path,
        'COLUMN_PREFIX': 'SAMPLE_',
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # One of: ['TEMPORARY_OUTPUT', 'Save to File…', 'Save to Geopackage…', 'Save to Database Table…']
    }

    result = processing.run("native:rastersampling", parameters)
  
    sampled_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Sampled Points', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(sampled_layer)

raster_sampling()

from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def calculate_surface_volume():
    input_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_html_path = 'path/to/output'

    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_layer_path, 'Elevation Data')

    # Define the parameters for the surface volume calculation
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'BAND': 1,  # Choose the band number. Type: [raster band] Default: The first band of the raster layer
        'LEVEL': 0.0,  # Define the base or reference value. Type: [number] Default: 0.0
        'METHOD': 0,  # Options: 0 — Above Base Level, 1 — Below Base Level, 2 — Subtract Below Base, 3 — Add Below Base. Default: 0
        'OUTPUT_HTML_FILE': output_html_path,  # Output HTML report. Type: [html] Default: [Save to temporary file]
        'OUTPUT_TABLE': 'SkipOutput'  # Output table. Type: [table] Default: [Skip output]
    }

    # Run the surface volume calculation algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:rastersurfacevolume', parameters)

    # Optionally, load the result to QGIS (if OUTPUT_TABLE is specified)
    if result.get('OUTPUT_TABLE') and result['OUTPUT_TABLE'] != 'SkipOutput':
        output_table_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT_TABLE'], 'Surface Volume Table', 'ogr')
        QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_table_layer)

# Execute the function
calculate_surface_volume()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def reclassify_raster_layer(input_raster_path, input_table_path, output_path):
    # Load the raster and table layers
    input_raster = QgsRasterLayer(input_raster_path, 'Input Raster')
    input_table = QgsVectorLayer(input_table_path, 'Input Table', 'ogr')

    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'INPUT_RASTER': input_raster,
        'RASTER_BAND': 1,  # Default: The first band of the raster layer
        'INPUT_TABLE': input_table,
        'MIN_FIELD': 'min_value',  # Field with the minimum value of the range for the class
        'MAX_FIELD': 'max_value',  # Field with the maximum value of the range for the class
        'VALUE_FIELD': 'output_value',  # Field with the value assigned to the pixels
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'NO_DATA': -9999.0,
        'RANGE_BOUNDARIES': 0,  # '0' = 'min < value <= max', '1' = 'min <= value < max', '2' = 'min <= value <= max', '3' = 'min < value < max'
        'NODATA_FOR_MISSING': False,
        'DATA_TYPE': 5  # '0' = Byte, '1' = Int16, '2' = UInt16, '3' = Int32, '4' = UInt32, '5' = Float32, '6' = Float64, '7' = CInt16, '8' = CInt32, '9' = CFloat32, '10' = CFloat64, '11' = Int8
    }

    # Execute the reclassification algorithm
    result = processing.run("""native:reclassifybylayer", parameters)

                                                           
    reclassified_raster_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Reclassified Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(reclassified_raster_layer)

output_path = '/path/to/output_reclassified.tif'

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsRasterLayer

def reclassify_raster(input_raster_path):
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    input_raster_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_raster_path, """Input Raster")

    parameters = {
        'INPUT_RASTER': input_raster_layer,
        'RASTER_BAND': 1,              
        'TABLE': [
            [-float('inf'), 50, 1],
            [50, 100, 2],
            [100, float('inf'), 3]
        ],
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'NO_DATA': -9999.0,                    
        'RANGE_BOUNDARIES': 0,                                                                                              
        'NODATA_FOR_MISSING': False,                  
        'DATA_TYPE': 5                                                                                                                                        
    }

    result = processing.run('native:reclassifybytable', parameters)
    
    reclassified_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Reclassified Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(reclassified_layer)

input_raster_path = "path/to/input_file"

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def refactor_fields():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    # Load the input layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, """Input Layer", "ogr")
    
                           
    fields_mapping = [{'expression': 'field_name', 'length': 255, 'name': 'new_field_name', 'precision': 0, 'type': 10}]                           
    
                                               
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'FIELDS_MAPPING': fields_mapping,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }
    
                                   
    result = processing.run("native:refactorfields", parameters)
    
                              
    refactored_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Refactored Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(refactored_layer)

                      
refactor_fields()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer

def remove_duplicates(input_layer_path):
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    duplicates_output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
   
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_layer_path, """Input Layer", "ogr")

    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'FIELDS': ['field1', 'field2'],
        'OUTPUT': output_path,                                                                                                        
        'DUPLICATES': duplicates_output_path                                                                                                                       
    }
    
    result = processing.run('native:removeduplicatesbyattribute', parameters)
    
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Unique Features', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)
    
    duplicates_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['DUPLICATES'], 'Duplicates Features', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(duplicates_layer)

remove_duplicates('path/to/input_file')

brief_description: Removes duplicate vertices from features, wherever removing the vertices does not result in a degenerate geometry.
full_description:
Removes duplicate vertices from features, wherever removing the vertices does not result in a degenerate geometry.
The tolerance parameter specifies the tolerance for coordinates when determining whether vertices are identical.
By default, Z values are not considered when detecting duplicate vertices. E.g. two vertices with the same X and Y coordinate but different Z values will still be considered duplicate and one will be removed. If the Use Z Value parameter is true, then the Z values are also tested and vertices with the same X and Y but different Z will be maintained.
Allows features in-place modification of point, line, and polygon features

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input vector layer.

parameters.name: TOLERANCE
parameters.label: Tolerance
parameters.description: Vertices closer than the specified distance are consideredduplicates.

parameters.name: USE_Z_VALUE
parameters.label: Use Z value
parameters.description: If the Use Z Value parameter is true, then the Zvalues are also tested and vertices with the same X and Y butdifferent Z will be maintained..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Cleaned
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

brief_description: Removes any features which do not have a geometry from a vector layer
full_description:
Removes any features which do not have a geometry from a vector layer. All other features will be copied unchanged.
The features with null geometries can be saved to a separate layer.
If Also remove empty geometries is checked, the algorithm removes features whose geometries have no coordinates, i.e., geometries that are empty. In that case, also the null output will reflect this option, containing both null and empty geometries.

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def remove_null_geometries():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
  
    # Load the input shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Remove Null Geometries algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:removenullgeometries', {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'REMOVE_EMPTY': True,  # Options: [True, False]
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'NULL_OUTPUT': 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT'  # Options: ['TEMPORARY_OUTPUT', 'Skip output', 'Save to File...', 'Save to Geopackage...', 'Save to Database Table...']
    })

    # Load the result layer into QGIS
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Non-null Geometries', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Execute the function
remove_null_geometries()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer

def reproject_layer(input_layer_path, output_path):
    # Load the input layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_layer_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer_path,
        'TARGET_CRS': 'EPSG:4326',  # Default CRS
        'CONVERT_CURVED_GEOMETRIES': False,  # Default value for optional parameter
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'OPERATION': ''  # Optional parameter if specific operation needed
    }

    # Run the reproject layer algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:reprojectlayer', parameters)
    
    # Load the reprojected layer into QGIS
    reprojected_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Reprojected Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(reprojected_layer)

# Define paths

output_path = "path/to/input_file"

brief_description: Rescales raster layer to a new value range, while preserving the shape (distribution) of the raster’s histogram (pixel values)
full_description:
Rescales raster layer to a new value range, while preserving the shape (distribution) of the raster’s histogram (pixel values). Input values are mapped using a linear interpolation from the source raster’s minimum and maximum pixel values to the destination minimum and miximum pixel range.
By default the algorithm preserves the original NoData value, but there is an option to override it.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input Raster
parameters.description: Raster layer to use for rescaling.

parameters.name: BAND
parameters.label: Band number
parameters.description: If the raster is multiband, choose a band..
parameters.default: The first band of the input layer

parameters.name: MINIMUM
parameters.label: New minimum value
parameters.description: Minimum pixel value to use in the rescaled layer.

parameters.name: MAXIMUM
parameters.label: New maximum value
parameters.description: Maximum pixel value to use in the rescaled layer.

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: New NoData value Optional
parameters.description: Value to assign to the NoData pixels.If unset, original NoData values are preserved..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Rescaled
parameters.description: Specification of the output raster layer.One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

# Import necessary modules from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject import processing

def rescale_raster():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
  
    # Load the raster file as a raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, 'Input Raster')

    # Run the Rescale Raster algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:rescaleraster', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'BAND': 1,  # Default: The first band of the input layer
        'MINIMUM': 0.0,
        'MAXIMUM': 255.0,
        'NODATA': None,  # Default value: Not set
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })

# Execute the function
rescale_raster()

brief_description: Calculates the roundness of each feature and stores it as a new field
full_description:
Calculates the roundness of each feature and stores it as a new field. The input vector layer must contain polygons.
The roundness of a polygon is defined as 4π × polygon area / perimeter². The roundness value varies between 0 and 1. A perfect circle has a roundness of 1, while a completely flat polygon has a roundness of 0.
Allows features in-place modification of polygon features

brief_description: Rounds the cell values of a raster dataset according to the specified number of decimals.
full_description:
Rounds the cell values of a raster dataset according to the specified number of decimals.
Alternatively, a negative number of decimal places may be used to round values to powers of a base n. For example, with a Base value n of 10 and Decimal places of -1, the algorithm rounds cell values to multiples of 10, -2 rounds to multiples of 100, and so on. Arbitrary base values may be chosen, the algorithm applies the same multiplicative principle. Rounding cell values to multiples of a base n may be used to generalize raster layers.
The algorithm preserves the data type of the input raster. Therefore byte/integer rasters can only be rounded to multiples of a base n, otherwise a warning is raised and the raster gets copied as byte/integer raster.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input raster
parameters.description: The raster to process..

parameters.name: BAND
parameters.label: Band number
parameters.description: The band of the raster.

parameters.name: ROUNDING_DIRECTION
parameters.label: Rounding direction
parameters.description: How to choose the target rounded value. Options are: 0 — Round up 1 — Round to nearest 2 — Round down.

parameters.name: DECIMAL_PLACES
parameters.label: Number of decimals places
parameters.description: Number of decimals places to round to.Use negative values to round cell values to a multiple of a base n.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Output raster
parameters.description: Specification of the output file. One of: Save to a Temporary File Save to File….

parameters.name: BASE_N
parameters.label: Base n for rounding to multiples of n
parameters.description: When the DECIMAL_PLACES parameter is negative,raster values are rounded to multiples of the base n value.

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems

# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def round_raster_values():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
  
    # Load the raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, 'Input Raster')

    # Run the Round Raster Values algorithm
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'BAND': 1,
        'ROUNDING_DIRECTION': 1,  # 0 — Round up, 1 — Round to nearest, 2 — Round down
        'DECIMAL_PLACES': 2,
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'BASE_N': 10
    }

    result = processing.run('native:roundrastervalues', parameters)

    # Load the rounded raster layer to QGIS
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Rounded Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Execute the function
round_raster_values()

# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsRasterLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def round_raster_values():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
  
    # Load the raster layer
    input_layer = QgsRasterLayer(input_path, 'Input Raster')

    # Run the Round Raster Values algorithm
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'BAND': 1,
        'ROUNDING_DIRECTION': 1,  # 0 — Round up, 1 — Round to nearest, 2 — Round down
        'DECIMAL_PLACES': 2,
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'BASE_N': 10
    }

    result = processing.run('native:roundrastervalues', parameters)

    # Load the rounded raster layer to QGIS
    output_layer = QgsRasterLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Rounded Raster')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Execute the function
round_raster_values()

original_code_example:
# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def save_features():
    # Define input and output paths
	input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
  
    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
	input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the save features algorithm
	result = processing.run('native:savefeatures', {
	'INPUT': input_path,
	'OUTPUT': output_path,
	'LAYER_NAME': 'Saved Layer',
	'DATASOURCE_OPTIONS': '',
	'LAYER_OPTIONS': '',
	'ACTION_ON_EXISTING_FILE': 0  # Options: [0, 1, 2, 3]
    })

    # Load the saved features layer to QGIS
	saved_features_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Saved Features', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(saved_features_layer)

# Execute the function
save_features()

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def save_selected_features():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    # Load the input layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')
    
    # Define the parameters for 'native:saveselectedfeatures'
    params = {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Options: Create Temporary Layer (TEMPORARY_OUTPUT), Save to File, Save to Geopackage, Save to Database Table
    }
    
    # Run the algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:saveselectedfeatures', params)
    
    # Load the resulting selected features layer to QGIS
    selected_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Selected Features', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(selected_layer)

# Execute the function
save_selected_features()

brief_description: Segmentizes a geometry by converting curved sections to linear sections.
full_description:
Segmentizes a geometry by converting curved sections to linear sections.
The segmentization is performed by specifying the maximum allowed radius angle between vertices on the straightened geometry (e.g the angle of the arc created from the original arc center to consecutive output vertices on the linearized geometry). Non-curved geometries will be retained without change.

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input line or polygon vector layer.

parameters.name: ANGLE
parameters.label: Maximum angle between vertices (degrees)
parameters.description: Maximum allowed radius angle between verticeson the straightened geometry.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Segmentized
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer (with segmentized geometries).One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def segmentize_by_max_angle():
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'  # specify the input layer path
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'  # specify the output layer path

    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'ANGLE': 5.0,  # default angle value
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer…
    }
    
    result = processing.run("""native:segmentizebymaxangle", parameters)
    
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Segmentized Geometries', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

segmentize_by_max_angle()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def select_within_distance():
    # Define input and reference layer paths
    input_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    reference_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the input and reference layers
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_layer_path, """Input Layer", "ogr")
    reference_layer = QgsVectorLayer(reference_layer_path, "Reference Layer", "ogr")

                                                                       
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer_path,
        'REFERENCE': reference_layer_path,
        'DISTANCE': 100,
        'METHOD': 0,                                                                                                                                                                         
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }

                                              
    result = processing.run("native:selectwithindistance", parameters)

                                               
    selected_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Selected_within_distance', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(selected_layer)

                      
select_within_distance()

The distance and the time (both referred to as "travel cost") must be specified respectively in the network layer units or in hours.

brief_description: Returns all the edges or parts of edges of a network that can be reached within a given distance or time, starting from a point feature
full_description: Returns all the edges or parts of edges of a network that can be reached within a given distance or time, starting from a point feature. This allows the evaluation of accessibility within a network, e.g. what are the places I can navigate to on a road network without spending a cost greater than a given value (the cost can be distance or time).

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Vector layer representing the network
parameters.description: Line vector layer representing the network to be covered.

parameters.name: START_POINT
parameters.label: Start point (x, y)
parameters.description: Coordinate of the point to calculate the servicearea around..

parameters.name: STRATEGY
parameters.label: Path type to calculate
parameters.description: The type of path to calculate. One of: 0 — Shortest 1 — Fastest.

parameters.name: TRAVEL_COST
parameters.label: Travel cost (distance for “Shortest”, time for “Fastest”)
parameters.description: The value is estimated as a distance (in the networklayer units) when looking for the Shortest path andas time (in hours) for the Fastest path..

parameters.name: OUTPUT_LINES
parameters.label: Service area (lines)
parameters.description: Specify the output line layer for the service area.One of: Skip Output Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Service area (boundary nodes)
parameters.description: Specify the output point layer for the service areaboundary nodes. One of: Skip Output Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

parameters.name: DIRECTION_FIELD
parameters.label: Direction field Optional
parameters.description: The field used to specify directions for the network edges. The values used in this field are specified with the threeparameters Value for forward direction , Value for backward direction and Value for both directions .Forward and reverse directions correspond to a one-way edge,“both directions” ind

parameters.name: VALUE_FORWARD
parameters.label: Value for forward direction Optional
parameters.description: Value set in the direction field to identify edges with aforward direction.

parameters.name: VALUE_BACKWARD
parameters.label: Value for backward direction Optional
parameters.description: Value set in the direction field to identify edges with abackward direction.

parameters.name: VALUE_BOTH
parameters.label: Value for both directions Optional
parameters.description: Value set in the direction field to identifybidirectional edges.

parameters.name: DEFAULT_DIRECTION
parameters.label: Default direction
parameters.description: If a feature has no value set in the direction field orif no direction field is set, then this direction valueis used. One of: 0 — Forward direction 1 — Backward direction 2 — Both directions.

parameters.name: SPEED_FIELD
parameters.label: Speed field Optional
parameters.description: Field providing the speed value (in km/h ) for theedges of the network when looking for the fastest path. If a feature does not have a value in this field, orno field is set then the default speed value (providedwith the Default speed parameter) is used..

parameters.name: DEFAULT_SPEED
parameters.label: Default speed (km/h)
parameters.description: Value to use to calculate the travel time if no speedfield is provided for an edge.

parameters.name: TOLERANCE
parameters.label: Topology tolerance
parameters.description: Two lines with nodes closer than the specifiedtolerance are considered connected.

use_cases: Service area and accessibility analysis
Routing and logistics optimization
Connectivity and infrastructure gap analysis
Emergency response time mapping

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def calculate_service_area():
    input_path = 'path/to/network_layer.shp'
    output_path = 'path/to/output_layer.shp'

    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, """Network Layer", "ogr")
    
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'START_POINT': 'x_coordinate, y_coordinate',
        'STRATEGY': 0,                                                
        'TRAVEL_COST': 0.0,
        'OUTPUT_LINES': 'TEMPORARY_OUTPUT',                     
        'OUTPUT': output_path,                                                 
        'DIRECTION_FIELD': '',
        'VALUE_FORWARD': '',
        'VALUE_BACKWARD': '',
        'VALUE_BOTH': '',
        'DEFAULT_DIRECTION': 2,                                                                                
        'SPEED_FIELD': '',
        'DEFAULT_SPEED': 50.0,
        'TOLERANCE': 0.0
    }

    result = processing.run("native:serviceareafrompoint", parameters)
    
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Service Area Boundary Nodes', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

calculate_service_area()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def apply_style_to_layer():
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    style_path = 'path/to/data'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, """Input Layer", "ogr")
    
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'STYLE': style_path,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }
    
    result = processing.run("native:setlayerstyle", parameters)
    
    styled_layer = QgsVectorLayer(output_path, 'Styled Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(styled_layer)

apply_style_to_layer()

brief_description: Uses values sampled from a band within a raster layer to set the M value for every overlapping vertex in the feature geometry
full_description:
Uses values sampled from a band within a raster layer to set the M value for every overlapping vertex in the feature geometry. The raster values can optionally be scaled by a preset amount.
If M values already exist in the layer, they will be overwritten with the new value. If no M values exist, the geometry will be upgraded to include M values.
Allows features in-place modification of point, line, and polygon features with M enabled

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input vector layer.

parameters.name: RASTER
parameters.label: Raster layer
parameters.description: Raster layer with M values.

parameters.name: BAND
parameters.label: Band number
parameters.description: The raster band from which the M values are taken.

parameters.name: NODATA
parameters.label: Value for NoData or non-intersecting vertices
parameters.description: Value to use in case the vertex does not intersect(a valid pixel of) the raster.

parameters.name: SCALE
parameters.label: Scale factor
parameters.description: Scaling value: the band values are multiplied by this value..

parameters.name: OFFSET
parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description: Offset value: it is algebraically added to the bandvalues after applying the “Scale factor”..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Updated
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer (with updated M values).One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

use_cases: Clipping national datasets to study area extents
Mosaicking tiled raster datasets (DEM tiles, NLCD, etc.)
Raster preprocessing for image analysis pipelines
Format conversion for interoperability with other systems

# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def set_m_from_raster():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    raster_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'RASTER': raster_path,
        'BAND': 1,  # Default band number
        'NODATA': 0.0,  # Default value for NoData
        'SCALE': 1.0,  # Default scale factor
        'OFFSET': 0.0,  # Default offset
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    }

    # Run the Set M from Raster algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:setmfromraster', parameters)

    # Load the updated layer to QGIS
    updated_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'UpdatedLayer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(updated_layer)

# Execute the function
set_m_from_raster()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer, QgsRasterLayer

def set_z_from_raster(input_layer_path, raster_layer_path, output_layer_path):
    # Load the vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_layer_path, 'Input Vector Layer', 'ogr')
    
    # Load the raster layer
    raster_layer = QgsRasterLayer(raster_layer_path, 'Input Raster Layer')
    
    # Define the parameters
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'RASTER': raster_layer,
        'BAND': 1,  # Default value
        'NODATA': 0,  # Default value
        'SCALE': 1.0,  # Default value
        'OFFSET': 0.0,  # Default value
        'OUTPUT': output_layer_path  # Specify the output layer path
    }

    # Run the Set Z from Raster algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:setzfromraster', parameters)

    # Load the updated layer to QGIS
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Updated Layer with Z Values', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Example usage
input_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
raster_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
output_layer_path = 'path/to/input_file'
set_z_from_raster(input_layer_path, raster_layer_path, output_layer_path)

Sets the Z value for geometries in a layer.
If Z values already exist in the layer, they will be overwritten with the new value. If no Z values exist, the geometry will be upgraded to include Z values and the specified value used as the initial Z value for all geometries.
Allows features in-place modification of point, line, and polygon features with Z enabled

brief_description: Creates a line layer as the shortest line between the source and the destination layer
full_description:
Creates a line layer as the shortest line between the source and the destination layer. By default only the first nearest feature of the destination layer is taken into account. The n-nearest neighboring features number can be specified. If a maximum distance is specified, then only features which are closer than this distance will be considered.
The output features will contain all the source layer attributes, all the attributes from the n-nearest feature and the additional field of the distance.

parameters.name: SOURCE
parameters.label: Source layer
parameters.description: Origin layer for which to search for nearest neighbors.

parameters.name: DESTINATION
parameters.label: Destination layer
parameters.description: Target Layer in which to search for nearest neighbors.

parameters.name: METHOD
parameters.label: Method
parameters.description: Shortest distance calculation methodPossible values are: 0 — Distance to nearest point on feature 1 — Distance to feature centroid.

parameters.name: NEIGHBORS
parameters.label: Maximum number of neighbors
parameters.description: Maximum number of neighbors to look for.

parameters.name: DISTANCE
parameters.label: Maximum distance Optional
parameters.description: Only destination features which are closer than this distancewill be considered..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Shortest lines
parameters.description: Specify the output vector layer. One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… The file encoding can also be changed here..

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer

def find_shortest_lines():
    # Define input and output paths
    source_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    destination_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefiles as vector layers
    source_layer = QgsVectorLayer(source_path, 'Source Layer', 'ogr')
    destination_layer = QgsVectorLayer(destination_path, 'Destination Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Shortest Line algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:shortestline', {
        'SOURCE': source_layer,
        'DESTINATION': destination_layer,
        'METHOD': 0,  # 0 — Distance to nearest point on feature, 1 — Distance to feature centroid
        'NEIGHBORS': 1,
        'DISTANCE': None,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })

    # Load the shortest lines layer to QGIS
    shortest_lines_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Shortest Lines', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(shortest_lines_layer)

# Execute the function
find_shortest_lines()

def find_shortest_path():
    # Import necessary QGIS and PyQt5 modules
    from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer
    import processing
    from PyQt5.QtCore import QVariant

    # Define paths to the input data
    network_points_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    network_lines_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_directory = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = f'{output_directory}/shortest_path.shp'

    # Load the network points and lines layers
    network_points_layer = QgsVectorLayer(network_points_path, 'Network Points', 'ogr')
    network_lines_layer = QgsVectorLayer(network_lines_path, 'Network Lines', 'ogr')

    # Define start and end points using the attributes in the network points layer
    # Assuming the first two points from the network_points_layer are City A and City B
    start_point = network_points_layer.getFeature(0).geometry().asPoint()
    end_point = network_points_layer.getFeature(1).geometry().asPoint()

    # Define the parameters for the shortest path (point to point) algorithm
    params = {
        'INPUT': network_lines_layer,  # Input is the network lines layer
        'START_POINT': f'{start_point.x()},{start_point.y()}',   # Start point geometry as string "x,y"
        'END_POINT': f'{end_point.x()},{end_point.y()}',         # End point geometry as string "x,y"
        'STRATEGY': 0,                # Strategy 0 means shortest path
        'OUTPUT': output_path         # Save the output to specified folder
    }

    # Execute the algorithm
    result = processing.run("native:shortestpathpointtopoint", params)

    # Load the output layer as a virtual layer within QGIS
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Shortest Path', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Execute the function to find and load the shortest path
find_shortest_path()

brief_description: Simplifies the geometries in a line or polygon layer
full_description:
Simplifies the geometries in a line or polygon layer. It creates a new layer with the same features as the ones in the input layer, but with geometries containing a lower number of vertices.
The algorithm gives a choice of simplification methods, including distance based (the “Douglas-Peucker” algorithm), area based (“Visvalingam” algorithm) and snapping geometries to grid.
Allows features in-place modification of line and polygon features
Default menu: Vector ► Geometry Tools

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input line or polygon vector layer.

parameters.name: METHOD
parameters.label: Simplification method
parameters.description: Simplification method. One of: 0 — Distance (Douglas-Peucker) 1 — Snap to grid 2 — Area (Visvalingam).

parameters.name: TOLERANCE
parameters.label: Tolerance
parameters.description: Threshold tolerance (in units of the layer):if the distance between two nodes is smaller thanthe tolerance value, the segment will be simplifiedand vertices will be removed..

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Simplified
parameters.description: Specify the output (simplified) vector layer.One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

# Import necessary modules
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def simplify_geometries():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
  
    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    # Run the Simplify Geometries algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:simplifygeometries', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'METHOD': 0,  # 0: Distance (Douglas-Peucker), 1: Snap to grid, 2: Area (Visvalingam)
        'TOLERANCE': 1.0,
        'OUTPUT': output_path
    })

    # Load the simplified layer to QGIS
    simplified_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Simplified', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(simplified_layer)

# Execute the function
simplify_geometries()

brief_description: Smooths the geometries in a line or polygon layer by adding more vertices and corners to the feature geometries.
full_description:
Smooths the geometries in a line or polygon layer by adding more vertices and corners to the feature geometries.
The iterations parameter dictates how many smoothing iterations will be applied to each geometry. A higher number of iterations results in smoother geometries with the cost of greater number of nodes in the geometries.
The offset parameter controls how “tightly” the smoothed geometries follow the original geometries. Smaller values results in a tighter fit, and larger values will create a looser fit.
The maximum angle parameter can be used to prevent smoothing of nodes with large angles. Any node where the angle of the segments to either side is larger than this will not be smoothed. For example, setting the maximum angle to 90 degrees or lower would preserve right angles in the geometry.
Allows features in-place modification of line and polygon features

parameters.name: INPUT
parameters.label: Input layer
parameters.description: Input line or polygon vector layer.

parameters.name: ITERATIONS
parameters.label: Iterations
parameters.description: Increasing the number of iterations will give smoothergeometries (and more vertices)..

parameters.name: OFFSET
parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description: Increasing values will move the smoothed lines / boundariesfurther away from the input lines / boundaries..

parameters.name: MAX_ANGLE
parameters.label: Maximum node angle to smooth
parameters.description: Every node below this value will be smoothed.

parameters.name: OUTPUT
parameters.label: Smoothed
parameters.description: Specify the output (smoothed) layer.One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

Terrain visualization for client deliverables

def smooth_geometry(input_layer_path):
    output_path = """path/to/input_file"

    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_layer_path, "Input Layer", "ogr")
    
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_layer_path,
        'ITERATIONS': 1,              
        'OFFSET': 0.25,                 
        'MAX_ANGLE': 180.0,                  
        'OUTPUT': output_path                                                                                                                                         
    }
    
    result = processing.run("native:smoothgeometry", parameters)
    
    smoothed_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Smoothed Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(smoothed_layer)

input_layer_path = "path/to/input_file"
smooth_geometry(input_layer_path)

import processing

def snap_geometries():
    # Define input, reference layer paths and output path
    input_path = """path/to/input_file"
    reference_layer_path = "path/to/input_file"
    output_path = "path/to/input_file"

                                                              
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, "Input Layer", "ogr")
    reference_layer = QgsVectorLayer(reference_layer_path, "Reference Layer", "ogr")

                           
    parameters = {

        'TOLERANCE': 10.0,
        'BEHAVIOR': 0,

    snapped_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Snapped Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(snapped_layer)

brief_description: Modifies the coordinates of geometries in a vector layer, so that all points or vertices are snapped to the closest point of a grid.
full_description:
Modifies the coordinates of geometries in a vector layer, so that all points or vertices are snapped to the closest point of a grid.
If the snapped geometry cannot be calculated (or is totally collapsed) the feature’s geometry will be cleared.
Snapping can be performed on the X, Y, Z or M axis. A grid spacing of 0 for any axis will disable snapping for that axis.
Allows features in-place modification of point, line, and polygon features

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsProject, QgsVectorLayer

def execute_sql_on_spatialite():
    # Define the database and SQL query
    database_path = 'path/to/data'
    sql_query = """UPDATE my_table SET field=10"

                                                            
    parameters = {
        'DATABASE': database_path,
        'SQL': sql_query,
                                                                              
        'OUTPUT': 'path/to/data'
    }

                                        
    result = processing.run("native:spatialiteexecutesql", parameters)
    
                                         
    if result['OUTPUT']:
        result_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Executed_SQL_Result', 'ogr')
        QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(result_layer)

                      
execute_sql_on_spatialite()

brief_description: Features are split into multiple output features by splitting a field’s value at a specified character
full_description:
Features are split into multiple output features by splitting a field’s value at a specified character. For instance, if a layer contains features with multiple comma separated values contained in a single field, this algorithm can be used to split these values up across multiple output features. Geometries and other attributes remain unchanged in the output. Optionally, the separator string can be a regular expression for added flexibility.
Allows features in-place modification of point, line, and polygon features

from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject
import processing

def split_lines_by_length():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'

    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Roads', 'ogr')

    # Run the Split Lines by Maximum Length algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:splitlinesbylength', {
        'INPUT': input_layer,
        'LENGTH': 10,  # Default value is 10.0
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # One of: Create Temporary Layer (TEMPORARY_OUTPUT), Save to File, Save to Geopackage, Save to Database Table, Append to Layer
    })

    # Load the split layer to QGIS
    split_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Split Roads', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(split_layer)

# Execute the function
split_lines_by_length()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def run_stdbscanclustering():
    # Define input and output paths
    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    # Load the shapefile as a vector layer
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')
    
    # Define the parameters dictionary
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'DATETIME_FIELD': 'timestamp',
        'MIN_SIZE': 5,
        'EPS': 1.0,
        'EPS2': '0.0 (days)',
        'OUTPUT': output_path,
        'DBSCAN*': False,
        'FIELD_NAME': 'CLUSTER_ID',
        'SIZE_FIELD_NAME': 'CLUSTER_SIZE'
    }
    
    # Run the stdbscanclustering algorithm
    result = processing.run('native:stdbscanclustering', parameters)
    
    # Load the result layer to QGIS
    output_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Standard DBSCAN Clusters', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(output_layer)

# Execute the function
run_stdbscanclustering()

brief_description: Subdivides the geometry

Subdivides the geometry. The returned geometry will be a collection containing subdivided parts from the original geometry, where no part has more than the specified maximum number of nodes.
This is useful for dividing a complex geometry into less complex parts, easier to spatially index and faster to perform spatial operations. Curved geometries will be segmentized before subdivision.
Allows features in-place modification of point, line, and polygon features

parameters.description: Specify the output (subdivided) vector layer.One of: Create Temporary Layer ( TEMPORARY_OUTPUT ) Save to File… Save to Geopackage… Save to Database Table… Append to Layer… The file encoding can also be changed here..

    input_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    output_path = 'path/to/input_file'
    
    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, 'Input Layer', 'ogr')

    # Define parameters for native:swapxy
    parameters = {
        'INPUT': input_path,
        'OUTPUT': output_path  # Create Temporary Layer (TEMPORARY_OUTPUT), Save to File…, Save to Geopackage…, Save to Database Table…, Append to Layer…
    }

    result = processing.run('native:swapxy', parameters)

    swapped_layer = QgsVectorLayer(result['OUTPUT'], 'Swapped Layer', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(swapped_layer)

swap_xy_coordinates()

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def tapered_buffer():
    input_path = """path/to/input_file"
    output_path = "path/to/input_file"

    input_layer = QgsVectorLayer(input_path, "Input Line Layer", "ogr")

        'END_WIDTH': 0.0,

import processing
from qgis.core import QgsVectorLayer, QgsProject

def create_mesh():
    # Define the input and output paths
    input_layers = ['path/to/input1.shp', 'path/to/input2.shp']
    output_path = """path/to/output_mesh.mesh"

                           
    input_layer_objects = [QgsVectorLayer(path, "Input Layer", "ogr") for path in input_layers]
    
                                           
    mesh_params = {
        'SOURCE_DATA': input_layers,
        'GUI ONLY: Value on vertex': 'field_name',
        'GUI ONLY: Use Z-coordinate for value on vertex': False,                 
        'MESH_FORMAT': 0,                                            
        'CRS_OUTPUT': None,                                           
        'OUTPUT_MESH': output_path
    }

                                   
    result = processing.run("native:tinmeshcreation", mesh_params)

                                 
    mesh_layer = QgsVectorLayer(output_path, 'Mesh Output', 'ogr')
    QgsProject.instance().addMapLayer(mesh_layer)

                  
create_mesh()
